questions.role: w − lr × m̂/√(v̂ + ε)

questions.question: Number of parameters in fully connected layer (n_in→n_out):
questions.role: n_in × n_out + n_out (weights + biases)

questions.question: Convolutional layer parameters: kernel k×k, in_channels C_in, out_channels C_out:
questions.role: k × k × C_in × C_out + C_out

questions.question: Output size of convolution: (W − F + 2P)/S + 1 where W=input, F=filter, P=padding, S=stride:
questions.role: Correct formula

questions.question: Total variation (TV) loss used for:
questions.role: Image smoothness regularization in style transfer / super-resolution

questions.question: What is the Bayes theorem in ML?
questions.role: P(A|B) = P(B|A)*P(A)/P(B) — basis for Naive Bayes classifier

questions.question: What is a Naive Bayes classifier?
questions.role: Probabilistic classifier assuming feature independence given class label

questions.question: What is the k-nearest neighbors algorithm?
questions.role: Classifies by majority vote of k nearest training examples

questions.question: What is a decision tree?
questions.role: Tree structure where each node is a feature split and leaves are predictions

questions.question: What is information gain in decision trees?
questions.role: Reduction in entropy after splitting on a feature

questions.question: What is Gini impurity?
questions.role: Probability of misclassifying a randomly chosen element: 1 - sum(p_i^2)

questions.question: What is entropy in decision trees?
questions.role: Measure of impurity/disorder: -sum(p_i * log2(p_i))

questions.question: What is Random Forest?
questions.role: Ensemble of decision trees trained on bootstrap samples with feature randomness

questions.question: What is feature importance in Random Forest?
questions.role: Average reduction in impurity across all trees for each feature

questions.question: What is out-of-bag (OOB) error?
questions.role: Error estimated on samples not used in training each tree (free validation)

questions.question: What is Gradient Boosting?
questions.role: Sequentially builds trees to correct residual errors of previous trees

questions.question: What is XGBoost?
questions.role: Extreme Gradient Boosting with regularization, parallel processing, and efficiency

questions.question: What is LightGBM?
questions.role: Gradient boosting using leaf-wise tree growth and histogram-based splits

questions.question: What is CatBoost?
questions.role: Gradient boosting with native categorical feature handling

questions.question: What is AdaBoost?
questions.role: Boosting algorithm assigning higher weights to misclassified examples

questions.question: What is support vector machine (SVM)?
questions.role: Finds optimal hyperplane maximizing margin between classes

questions.question: What is the kernel trick in SVM?
questions.role: Maps features to higher dimension implicitly via kernel function

questions.question: What are common kernels in SVM?
questions.role: Linear, RBF (Gaussian), Polynomial, Sigmoid

questions.question: What is the C parameter in SVM?
questions.role: Regularization: low C=wider margin+more violations; high C=narrow margin+fewer violations

questions.question: What is the gamma parameter in RBF kernel?
questions.role: Controls influence range of training examples — high gamma=small influence

questions.question: What is logistic regression?
questions.role: Linear model for binary classification using sigmoid function

questions.question: What is softmax function?
questions.role: Generalizes sigmoid to multi-class: converts logits to probability distribution

questions.question: What is cross-entropy loss for classification?
questions.role: Measures difference between predicted probability distribution and true labels

questions.question: What is the KL divergence?
questions.role: Measures how one probability distribution differs from another

questions.question: What is the chi-squared test for features?
questions.role: Tests independence between categorical feature and target label

questions.question: What is ANOVA F-test for features?
questions.role: Tests if feature means differ across class labels for feature selection

questions.question: What is recursive feature elimination (RFE)?
questions.role: Recursively removes least important features until target count reached

questions.question: What is the Lasso for feature selection?
questions.role: L1 regularization drives unimportant feature coefficients to zero

questions.question: What is ElasticNet?
questions.role: Combines L1 and L2 regularization for feature selection and weight reduction

questions.question: What is clustering?
questions.role: Grouping similar data points without labels (unsupervised learning)

questions.question: What is k-means clustering?
questions.role: Assigns points to k centroids, updates centroids iteratively until convergence

questions.question: What is the elbow method for k-means?
questions.role: Plot inertia vs k — choose k where improvement slows significantly

questions.question: What is the silhouette score?
questions.role: Measures how similar a point is to its cluster vs nearest cluster

questions.question: What is DBSCAN?
questions.role: Density-based clustering finding arbitrary-shaped clusters and outliers

questions.question: What is hierarchical clustering?
questions.role: Creates cluster tree (dendrogram) by merging or splitting clusters

questions.question: What is the linkage criterion in hierarchical clustering?
questions.role: How distance between clusters is measured: single, complete, average, Ward

questions.question: What is Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM)?
questions.role: Probabilistic model assuming data is mixture of Gaussian distributions

questions.question: What is the EM algorithm?
questions.role: Expectation-Maximization for learning parameters of latent variable models

questions.question: What is Isolation Forest?
questions.role: Anomaly detection isolating outliers using random feature splits

questions.question: What is Local Outlier Factor (LOF)?
questions.role: Anomaly detection comparing local density of point to its neighbors

questions.question: What is one-class SVM?
questions.role: Learns boundary around normal data for anomaly detection

questions.question: What is auto-encoder for anomaly detection?
questions.role: Learns to reconstruct normal data — high reconstruction error = anomaly

questions.question: What is time series forecasting?
questions.role: Predicting future values based on historical sequential data

questions.question: What is the ADF test?
questions.role: Augmented Dickey-Fuller test for stationarity of time series

questions.question: What is differentiable NAS (DARTS)?
questions.role: Uses continuous relaxation and gradient descent for architecture search

questions.question: What is hardware-aware NAS?
questions.role: Optimizes for inference speed on target device alongside accuracy

questions.question: What is the purpose of a learning rate warmup?
questions.role: Avoid large unstable updates at start with randomly initialized weights

questions.question: What is the cosine learning rate decay formula?
questions.role: lr = lr_min + 0.5*(lr_max-lr_min)*(1+cos(pi*step/total_steps))

questions.question: What is gradient penalty?
questions.role: Adds penalty for gradient norm to enforce Lipschitz constraint in WGAN

questions.question: What is the Lipschitz constraint?
questions.role: Restricts function's rate of change — used in WGAN for stable training

questions.question: What is spectral normalization?
questions.role: Normalize weight matrices by their spectral norm for Lipschitz continuity

questions.question: What is layer normalization?
questions.role: Normalizes across features for a single sample (vs batch norm across batch)

questions.question: What is instance normalization?
questions.role: Normalizes each sample's spatial dimensions independently — used in style transfer

questions.question: What is group normalization?
questions.role: Divides channels into groups and normalizes within each group

questions.question: What is AdaGrad optimizer?
questions.role: Adapts learning rate per parameter using accumulated squared gradients

questions.question: What is RMSProp optimizer?
questions.role: Like AdaGrad but uses exponential moving average to prevent shrinking LR

questions.question: What is AdamW optimizer?
questions.role: Adam with decoupled weight decay — better generalization than standard Adam

questions.question: What is the weight decay decoupling in AdamW?
questions.role: Applies weight decay to weights directly, not through gradient (different from L2)

questions.question: What is Lion optimizer?
questions.role: Sign-based optimizer more memory-efficient than Adam

questions.question: What is the Prodigy optimizer?
questions.role: Automatically estimates learning rate from data without tuning

questions.question: What is continual learning?
questions.role: Model learns new tasks without forgetting previous ones (catastrophic forgetting)

questions.question: What is replay-based continual learning?
questions.role: Store samples from previous tasks and replay during new task training

questions.question: What is few-shot learning?
questions.role: Learning to classify with very few labeled examples per class

questions.question: What is zero-shot learning?
questions.role: Classify unseen classes using semantic descriptions without examples

questions.question: What is meta-learning?
questions.role: Learning to learn — model adapts quickly to new tasks from few examples

questions.question: What is MAML (Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning)?
questions.role: Finds initialization that adapts quickly to new tasks with few gradient steps

questions.question: What is contrastive learning?
questions.role: Learns representations by bringing similar examples close, pushing dissimilar apart

questions.question: What is SimCLR?
questions.role: Contrastive learning framework using augmented views of same image

questions.question: What is BYOL (Bootstrap Your Own Latent)?
questions.role: Self-supervised learning without negative samples using online/target network

questions.question: What is Barlow Twins?
questions.role: Self-supervised method reducing redundancy between embedding dimensions

questions.question: What is supervised contrastive learning?
questions.role: Uses label information to group same-class samples in embedding space

questions.question: What is the bias in a neural network?
questions.role: Learnable parameter added to weighted sum before activation

questions.question: What is the universal approximation theorem?
questions.role: A feedforward network with one hidden layer can approximate any continuous function

questions.question: What is semi-supervised learning?
questions.role: Uses small labeled dataset and large unlabeled dataset together

questions.question: What is self-supervised learning?
questions.role: Creates labels from data itself (predict masked tokens, next frame)

questions.question: What is the manifold hypothesis?
questions.role: Real-world data lies on low-dimensional manifold in high-dimensional space

questions.question: What is the Occam's razor principle in ML?
questions.role: Prefer simpler models when they explain data equally well

questions.question: What is a calibrated model?
questions.role: Predicted probability 0.7 means 70% of such predictions are actually correct

questions.question: What is the F-beta score?
questions.role: Weighted harmonic mean of precision and recall: beta>1 weights recall more

questions.question: What is stratified k-fold cross-validation?
questions.role: Preserves class distribution in each fold — better for imbalanced datasets

questions.question: What is the imbalanced dataset problem?
questions.role: Model biased toward majority class — use oversampling, undersampling, or class weights

questions.question: What is class weight in ML?
questions.role: Penalizes misclassification of minority class more during training

questions.question: What is the precision-recall tradeoff?
questions.role: Increasing threshold raises precision but lowers recall and vice versa

questions.question: What is isotonic regression for calibration?
questions.role: Non-parametric method mapping predicted scores to calibrated probabilities

questions.question: What is the dummy classifier?
questions.role: Baseline classifier using simple rules (most frequent, random) to compare against

questions.question: What is the Matthews Correlation Coefficient?
questions.role: Balanced metric for binary classification considering all four confusion matrix cells

questions.question: What is Cohen's kappa?
questions.role: Measures agreement between predicted and actual labels accounting for chance

questions.question: What is the Brier score?
questions.role: Mean squared error of probabilistic predictions — lower is better

questions.question: What is log loss (binary cross-entropy)?
questions.role: Penalizes confident wrong predictions more than uncertain wrong ones

questions.question: What is the hinge loss?
questions.role: Used in SVM: max(0, 1 - y*f(x)) — zero loss for correct confident predictions

questions.question: What is focal loss?
questions.role: Down-weights easy examples so model focuses on hard cases (used in object detection)

questions.question: What is dice loss?
questions.role: Overlap measure between predicted and true segmentation masks

questions.question: What is multi-label classification?
questions.role: Each sample can belong to multiple classes simultaneously

questions.question: What is ordinal regression?
questions.role: Predicts ordered categories (low/medium/high) respecting natural ordering

questions.question: What is regression to the mean?
questions.role: Extreme observations tend to be followed by more average ones

questions.question: What is Pearson correlation limitation?
questions.role: Only measures linear relationships — misses non-linear dependencies

questions.question: What is Spearman correlation?
questions.role: Rank-based correlation detecting monotonic (not just linear) relationships

questions.question: What is the central limit theorem relevance to ML?
questions.role: Large samples from any distribution have normally distributed means — justifies many ML assumptions

questions.question: What is gradient-free optimization?
questions.role: Evolutionary algorithms, particle swarm — no gradient needed

questions.question: What is the lottery ticket hypothesis?
questions.role: Dense networks contain sparse subnetworks (winning tickets) that train effectively alone

questions.question: What is magnitude pruning?
questions.role: Remove weights with smallest absolute values for model compression

questions.question: What is structured pruning?
questions.role: Remove entire filters/layers rather than individual weights

questions.question: What is quantization-aware training benefit?
questions.role: Model learns to be robust to quantization during training — better accuracy than post-training

questions.question: What is the straight-through estimator?
questions.role: Approximates gradient of discrete/non-differentiable operations for backprop

questions.question: What is neural network surgery?
questions.role: Modifying trained network architecture (add layers, change connections)

questions.question: What is the purpose of a validation curve?
questions.role: Shows how a hyperparameter affects train vs validation performance

questions.question: What is the double descent phenomenon?
questions.role: Test error decreases, then increases (classical overfitting), then decreases again with more params

questions.question: What is grokking in deep learning?
questions.role: Model suddenly generalizes long after achieving perfect training accuracy

questions.question: What is Simpson's paradox in ML?
questions.role: Trend appears in groups but reverses when groups are combined — data aggregation issue

questions.question: What is causal inference vs correlation?
questions.role: Causal: establishes cause-effect; Correlation: only association

questions.question: What is propensity score matching?
questions.role: Controls for confounders in observational studies to estimate causal effect

questions.question: What is an instrumental variable?
questions.role: Variable correlated with treatment but not outcome except through treatment — enables causal inference

questions.question: What is A/B testing in ML context?
questions.role: Controlled experiment comparing two model versions on real users

questions.question: What is the multiple comparisons problem?
questions.role: Running many statistical tests inflates false positive rate

questions.question: What is Bonferroni correction?
questions.role: Divide significance threshold by number of tests to control family-wise error rate

questions.question: What is bootstrap confidence interval?
questions.role: Resample data with replacement many times to estimate sampling distribution

questions.question: What is effect size in ML experiments?
questions.role: Magnitude of difference between models — statistical significance doesn't guarantee practical importance

questions.question: What is Bayesian A/B testing?
questions.role: Compute posterior probability one variant is better instead of p-values

questions.question: What is power analysis?
questions.role: Determines minimum sample size needed to detect an effect with given probability

questions.question: What is the type I error (false positive)?
questions.role: Rejecting null hypothesis when it's actually true

questions.question: What is the type II error (false negative)?
questions.role: Failing to reject null hypothesis when it's actually false

questions.question: What is the p-value?
questions.role: Probability of observing results as extreme as current ones if null hypothesis is true

questions.question: What is the reproducibility crisis relevance to ML?
questions.role: Many ML results don't reproduce due to random seeds, data splits, implementation details

questions.question: What is the generalization gap?
questions.role: Difference between training performance and test performance

questions.question: What is the Vapnik-Chervonenkis (VC) dimension?
questions.role: Measure of capacity/expressiveness of a hypothesis class

questions.question: What is the empirical risk minimization?
questions.role: Minimize loss on training data as proxy for true risk minimization

questions.question: What is Rademacher complexity?
questions.role: Measures model class's ability to fit random noise — bounds generalization error

questions.question: What is transfer learning benefit?
questions.role: Pretrained features from source task accelerate learning on target task

questions.question: What is domain adaptation?
questions.role: Transferring model from source domain to related target domain with distribution shift

questions.question: What is label shift (prior probability shift)?
questions.role: Class distribution differs between train and test but P(x|y) is the same

questions.question: What is importance weighting for covariate shift?
questions.role: Reweight training samples by ratio of test to train density to correct bias

questions.question: What is rejection sampling?
questions.role: Generate samples from target distribution using easier proposal distribution

questions.question: What is the curse of dimensionality for density estimation?
questions.role: Exponentially more data needed to cover high-dimensional space

questions.question: What is kernel density estimation (KDE)?
questions.role: Non-parametric method estimating probability density using kernel functions

questions.question: What is the bandwidth parameter in KDE?
questions.role: Controls smoothness: small=wiggly (overfit), large=smooth (underfit)

questions.question: What is model ensembling at test time?
questions.role: Average predictions from multiple models for better accuracy and calibration

questions.question: What is Docker?
questions.role: Platform for packaging applications into portable containers

questions.question: What is a Docker container?
questions.role: Lightweight isolated runtime environment sharing host OS kernel

questions.question: What is a Docker image?
questions.role: Read-only template for creating containers

questions.question: What is a Dockerfile?
questions.role: Script with instructions to build a Docker image

questions.question: What does FROM in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Specifies the base image for the build

questions.question: What does RUN in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Executes commands in a new layer during build

questions.question: What does COPY in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Copies files from host to image filesystem

questions.question: What does CMD in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Default command to run when container starts

questions.question: What is the difference between CMD and ENTRYPOINT?
questions.role: ENTRYPOINT is always executed; CMD provides default arguments

questions.question: What does EXPOSE in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Documents which port container listens on (doesn't actually publish)

questions.question: What does ENV in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Sets environment variable in the image

questions.question: What does WORKDIR in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Sets the working directory for subsequent instructions

questions.question: What is a Docker volume?
questions.role: Persistent storage that exists outside container lifecycle

questions.question: What is a bind mount?
questions.role: Mounts a host filesystem path into the container

questions.question: What does docker build do?
questions.role: Builds a Docker image from a Dockerfile

questions.question: What does docker run do?
questions.role: Creates and starts a container from an image

questions.question: What does -d flag in docker run do?
questions.role: Runs container in detached (background) mode

questions.question: What does -p 8080:80 flag do?
questions.role: Maps host port 8080 to container port 80

questions.question: What does docker ps do?
questions.role: Lists running containers

questions.question: What does docker ps -a do?
questions.role: Lists all containers (running and stopped)

questions.question: What does docker images do?
questions.role: Lists locally stored images

questions.question: What does docker pull do?
questions.role: Downloads image from registry

questions.question: What does docker push do?
questions.role: Uploads image to a registry

questions.question: What is Docker Hub?
questions.role: Public registry for Docker images

questions.question: What does docker exec do?
questions.role: Runs a command inside running container

questions.question: What does docker logs do?
questions.role: Shows container stdout/stderr logs

questions.question: What does docker stop do?
questions.role: Gracefully stops a running container (SIGTERM)

questions.question: What does docker rm do?
questions.role: Removes a stopped container

questions.question: What does docker rmi do?
questions.role: Removes a Docker image

questions.question: What is Docker Compose?
questions.role: Tool for defining multi-container apps with docker-compose.yml

questions.question: What is the purpose of docker-compose.yml?
questions.role: Defines services, networks, and volumes for multi-container apps

questions.question: What does docker-compose up do?
questions.role: Creates and starts all services in compose file

questions.question: What does docker-compose down do?
questions.role: Stops and removes containers, networks, and volumes

questions.question: What is a Docker network?
questions.role: Virtual network enabling containers to communicate

questions.question: What are the default Docker network drivers?
questions.role: bridge, host, none, overlay

questions.question: What is the bridge network in Docker?
questions.role: Default network for containers on single host

questions.question: What is overlay network in Docker?
questions.role: Multi-host network for Docker Swarm services

questions.question: What is Docker layer caching?
questions.role: Reusing unchanged Dockerfile layers to speed up builds

questions.question: Why should you order Dockerfile instructions from least to most frequently changed?
questions.role: To maximize layer cache utilization

questions.question: What is a multi-stage Dockerfile build?
questions.role: Using multiple FROM statements to create smaller production images

questions.question: What is the difference between image and container?
questions.role: Image is template; container is running instance of image

questions.question: What is Docker Swarm?
questions.role: Native Docker cluster management and orchestration tool

questions.question: What is the difference between Docker Swarm and Kubernetes?
questions.role: K8s is more powerful with larger ecosystem; Swarm is simpler

questions.question: What is a Docker registry?
questions.role: Storage and distribution system for Docker images

questions.question: What is a private registry?
questions.role: Registry accessible only within organization (e.g., ECR, GCR)

questions.question: What does .dockerignore do?
questions.role: Excludes files/directories from the build context

questions.question: What is the HEALTHCHECK instruction?
questions.role: Defines command Docker uses to check if container is healthy

questions.question: What is docker inspect?
questions.role: Returns detailed JSON information about Docker objects

questions.question: What is docker stats?
questions.role: Shows live resource usage (CPU, memory) of containers

questions.question: What is the principle of immutable containers?
questions.role: Containers should not be modified in place — rebuild and redeploy

questions.question: What is a scratch image?
questions.role: Minimal empty base image for building from scratch

questions.question: What does USER instruction in Dockerfile do?
questions.role: Sets the user for running subsequent commands (security best practice)

questions.question: What is container orchestration?
questions.role: Automating deployment, scaling, and management of containers

questions.question: What is tini?
questions.role: Minimal init system to handle PID 1 signal handling in containers

questions.question: What is a Docker socket?
questions.role: Unix socket (/var/run/docker.sock) for communicating with Docker daemon

questions.question: What is the Docker daemon?
questions.role: Background service managing Docker objects (images, containers, networks)

questions.question: What is context in docker build?
questions.role: Files sent to Docker daemon for building (current directory by default)

questions.question: What is the ADD instruction vs COPY?
questions.role: ADD can untar archives and fetch URLs; COPY is simpler and preferred

questions.question: What is docker prune?
questions.role: Removes unused Docker objects (containers, images, networks, volumes)

questions.question: What is the difference between docker stop and docker kill?
questions.role: stop sends SIGTERM; kill sends SIGKILL immediately

questions.question: What is a container restart policy?
questions.role: Configures whether/when Docker auto-restarts containers (always, on-failure)

questions.question: What is dind (Docker in Docker)?
questions.role: Running Docker daemon inside a Docker container

questions.question: What is a read-only container?
questions.role: Container with --read-only flag, filesystem cannot be written to

questions.question: What is Docker content trust?
questions.role: Signs and verifies Docker images for security

questions.question: What is a Docker plugin?
questions.role: Extension adding functionality to Docker daemon (logging, volumes)

questions.question: What is --no-cache in docker build?
questions.role: Disables layer caching, performs fresh build from scratch

questions.question: What is the purpose of ARG in Dockerfile?
questions.role: Defines build-time variables passed via --build-arg

questions.question: What is the difference between ARG and ENV in Dockerfile?
questions.role: ARG is build-time only; ENV persists in the image and runtime

questions.question: What is a Docker namespace?
questions.role: Linux feature providing process isolation for containers

questions.question: What is a cgroup?
questions.role: Linux feature limiting resource (CPU, memory) usage for containers

questions.question: What is seccomp in Docker security?
questions.role: System call filtering to restrict container capabilities

questions.question: What is a multi-arch image?
questions.role: Single image tag serving different CPU architectures (amd64, arm64)

questions.question: What is ONBUILD instruction?
questions.role: Adds trigger instruction to execute when image is used as base

questions.question: What does docker tag do?
questions.role: Creates a new tag for an existing image

questions.question: What is Docker's copy-on-write filesystem?
questions.role: Containers share image layers and only write differences

questions.question: What is a LABEL in Dockerfile?
questions.role: Adds metadata key-value pairs to image (author, version, etc.)

questions.question: What is the init flag in docker run?
questions.role: Runs init process as PID 1 for proper signal handling

questions.question: What is container drift?
questions.role: Production container having different state than original image

questions.question: What is Distroless image?
questions.role: Minimal image containing only application and runtime, no shell

questions.question: What is BuildKit?
questions.role: Next-generation Docker build engine with better performance and security

questions.question: What is the purpose of STOPSIGNAL in Dockerfile?
questions.role: Sets the signal sent to container to stop it

questions.question: What is docker commit?
questions.role: Creates image from running container's current state (anti-pattern)

questions.question: What is a tmpfs mount?
questions.role: Temporary filesystem stored in host memory, not persisted

questions.question: What is container image scanning?
questions.role: Checking container images for known security vulnerabilities

questions.question: What tool is commonly used for image scanning?
questions.role: Trivy or Snyk

questions.question: What is OCI (Open Container Initiative)?
questions.role: Standards for container formats and runtimes

questions.question: What is containerd?
questions.role: Industry-standard container runtime used by Kubernetes

questions.question: What is runc?
questions.role: Low-level OCI container runtime used by containerd

questions.question: What is the difference between docker run -v and --mount?
questions.role: --mount is more explicit and recommended; -v is shorthand

questions.question: What is a named volume vs anonymous volume?
questions.role: Named has user-defined name; anonymous has Docker-generated hash

questions.question: What is volume driver?
questions.role: Plugin for storing volumes on external storage (NFS, cloud)

questions.question: What does docker volume prune do?
questions.role: Removes all unused (not mounted by any container) volumes

questions.question: What is docker save and docker load?
questions.role: Save exports image to tar; load imports tar as image

questions.question: What is a distro-specific image tag (e.g., alpine, slim)?
questions.role: Alpine/slim are minimal variants; alpine uses musl libc

questions.question: What is Kubernetes (K8s)?
questions.role: Open-source container orchestration platform for automating deployment and scaling

questions.question: What is a Pod in Kubernetes?
questions.role: Smallest deployable unit, one or more containers sharing network/storage

questions.question: What is a Node in Kubernetes?
questions.role: Physical or virtual machine in the cluster running Pods

questions.question: What is a Cluster in Kubernetes?
questions.role: Group of nodes managed by Kubernetes control plane

questions.question: What is the Control Plane?
questions.role: Set of components managing the cluster (API server, etcd, scheduler, controller)

questions.question: What is the API server in K8s?
questions.role: Front-end for K8s control plane, processes REST API requests

questions.question: What is etcd?
questions.role: Distributed key-value store used as K8s cluster backing store

questions.question: What is the Scheduler in K8s?
questions.role: Assigns unscheduled Pods to nodes based on resource availability

questions.question: What is a Deployment in K8s?
questions.role: Manages ReplicaSet ensuring desired number of identical Pods running

questions.question: What is a ReplicaSet?
questions.role: Ensures a specified number of Pod replicas are running at all times

questions.question: What is a Service in K8s?
questions.role: Stable network endpoint with DNS name to access a set of Pods

questions.question: What is ClusterIP service?
questions.role: Exposes service on internal cluster IP (default, not externally accessible)

questions.question: What is NodePort service?
questions.role: Exposes service on each Node's IP at a static port

questions.question: What is LoadBalancer service?
questions.role: Provisions external load balancer from cloud provider to expose service

questions.question: What is Ingress in K8s?
questions.role: API object managing external HTTP/HTTPS access to services in cluster

questions.question: What is a Namespace in K8s?
questions.role: Virtual cluster for organizing and isolating resources within a cluster

questions.question: What is a ConfigMap?
questions.role: Stores non-confidential configuration data as key-value pairs

questions.question: What is a Secret in K8s?
questions.role: Stores sensitive data like passwords and tokens, base64-encoded

questions.question: What is a PersistentVolume (PV)?
questions.role: Storage resource provisioned by admin or dynamically

questions.question: What is a PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC)?
questions.role: Request for storage by a user, binds to a matching PV

questions.question: What is a StatefulSet?
questions.role: Like Deployment but for stateful apps with stable IDs and storage

questions.question: What is a DaemonSet?
questions.role: Ensures one Pod copy runs on each (or specific) node

questions.question: What is a Job in K8s?
questions.role: Runs a Pod to completion (one-off task)

questions.question: What is a CronJob in K8s?
questions.role: Runs Jobs on a time-based schedule

questions.question: What is kubectl?
questions.role: CLI tool for interacting with Kubernetes clusters

questions.question: What does kubectl get pods do?
questions.role: Lists all Pods in current namespace

questions.question: What does kubectl apply -f do?
questions.role: Creates or updates resources defined in YAML file

questions.question: What does kubectl describe pod do?
questions.role: Shows detailed information about a specific Pod

questions.question: What does kubectl logs do?
questions.role: Shows container logs from a Pod

questions.question: What does kubectl exec -it do?
questions.role: Opens interactive terminal inside a running container

questions.question: What is a liveness probe?
questions.role: Checks if container is still running; restarts if failed

questions.question: What is a readiness probe?
questions.role: Checks if container is ready to receive traffic

questions.question: What is a startup probe?
questions.role: Checks if application has started; disables liveness during startup

questions.question: What is resource requests in K8s?
questions.role: Minimum resources guaranteed to a container

questions.question: What is resource limits in K8s?
questions.role: Maximum resources a container can use

questions.question: What is Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA)?
questions.role: Automatically scales Pod replicas based on CPU/memory metrics

questions.question: What is Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA)?
questions.role: Adjusts CPU/memory requests of running Pods

questions.question: What is Cluster Autoscaler?
questions.role: Automatically adds/removes nodes based on pending Pods and utilization

questions.question: What is a Helm chart?
questions.role: Package of pre-configured K8s resources for easy deployment

questions.question: What is Helm?
questions.role: Kubernetes package manager for templating and managing releases

questions.question: What is a taint in K8s?
questions.role: Mark on node preventing unacceptable Pods from scheduling on it

questions.question: What is a toleration in K8s?
questions.role: Allows a Pod to schedule on a node with a matching taint

questions.question: What is a node affinity?
questions.role: Rules preferring or requiring pods to be scheduled on specific nodes

questions.question: What is pod affinity/anti-affinity?
questions.role: Rules scheduling pods near or away from other pods

questions.question: What is RBAC in Kubernetes?
questions.role: Role-Based Access Control for managing permissions

questions.question: What is a ServiceAccount?
questions.role: Identity for pods to authenticate with K8s API

questions.question: What is a NetworkPolicy?
questions.role: Rules controlling traffic flow between Pods and namespaces

questions.question: What is kube-proxy?
questions.role: Maintains network rules on nodes for service communication

questions.question: What is CoreDNS in K8s?
questions.role: Default DNS server providing service discovery via DNS names

questions.question: What is Istio?
questions.role: Service mesh for K8s providing traffic management, security, observability

questions.question: What is a sidecar container?
questions.role: Additional container in a Pod sharing resources with main container

questions.question: What is an init container?
questions.role: Specialized container running before app containers in a Pod

questions.question: What is K8s rolling update?
questions.role: Gradually replaces old Pods with new ones to avoid downtime

questions.question: What is blue-green deployment?
questions.role: Two identical environments — switch traffic from blue to green

questions.question: What is canary deployment?
questions.role: Gradually routing a small percentage of traffic to new version

questions.question: What is etcd backup?
questions.role: Critical operation protecting cluster state and configuration

questions.question: What is kubectl port-forward?
questions.role: Forwards local port to Pod port for debugging

questions.question: What is K8s RBAC Role vs ClusterRole?
questions.role: Role is namespace-scoped; ClusterRole is cluster-wide

questions.question: What is a ResourceQuota?
questions.role: Limits total resource consumption within a namespace

questions.question: What is LimitRange?
questions.role: Sets default resource limits and constraints for containers in namespace

questions.question: What does kubectl rollout undo do?
questions.role: Rolls back Deployment to previous revision

questions.question: What is a K8s Operator?
questions.role: Custom controller extending K8s to manage complex stateful applications

questions.question: What is Custom Resource Definition (CRD)?
questions.role: Extends K8s API with custom resource types

questions.question: What is kubeconfig?
questions.role: Configuration file with cluster credentials and context for kubectl

questions.question: What is a K8s context?
questions.role: Named combination of cluster, user, and namespace for kubectl

questions.question: What is pod disruption budget (PDB)?
questions.role: Minimum available pods during voluntary disruptions (e.g., upgrades)

questions.question: What is K8s federation?
questions.role: Managing multiple K8s clusters as one

questions.question: What is Kustomize?
questions.role: K8s native configuration management using overlay patches

questions.question: What is ArgoCD?
questions.role: GitOps continuous delivery tool for K8s

questions.question: What is Flux?
questions.role: GitOps operator for K8s reconciling cluster state with Git

questions.question: What is the purpose of namespaces in K8s multi-tenancy?
questions.role: Isolate teams/environments sharing same cluster

questions.question: What is K8s pod lifecycle: Pending state?
questions.role: Pod accepted but containers not yet running (waiting for scheduling/image pull)

questions.question: What is K8s pod status CrashLoopBackOff?
questions.role: Container keeps crashing and K8s is backing off restart attempts

questions.question: What is K8s pod status ImagePullBackOff?
questions.role: K8s can't pull container image (wrong name, auth, network)

questions.question: What is OOM (Out of Memory) kill in K8s?
questions.role: Container exceeds memory limit and is killed by kernel

questions.question: What is kube-scheduler's bin-packing vs spreading?
questions.role: Bin-packing fills nodes densely; spreading distributes across nodes

questions.question: What is Priority Class in K8s?
questions.role: Assigns scheduling priority to Pods; high-priority pods preempt low ones

questions.question: What is eviction in K8s?
questions.role: Removing pods from nodes under resource pressure

questions.question: What is K8s API versioning (e.g., v1, apps/v1)?
questions.role: Different stability levels: core (v1), extensions (apps/v1, etc.)

questions.question: What is kubectl top?
questions.role: Shows resource (CPU/memory) usage of pods/nodes

questions.question: What is a K8s Admission Controller?
questions.role: Plugin intercepting API requests before persistence to validate/mutate

questions.question: What is OPA (Open Policy Agent) in K8s?
questions.role: Policy engine enforcing fine-grained access control

questions.question: What is Gatekeeper?
questions.role: OPA admission controller for K8s policy enforcement

questions.question: What is GitOps?
questions.role: Using Git as single source of truth; automation reconciles cluster to Git state

questions.question: What is pull-based vs push-based GitOps?
questions.role: Pull: operator watches Git and pulls changes; Push: CI pushes to cluster

questions.question: What is Velero?
questions.role: Backup and restore tool for Kubernetes cluster resources and volumes

questions.question: What is the difference between K8s StatefulSet and Deployment for databases?
questions.role: StatefulSet provides stable network IDs and persistent storage per pod

questions.question: What is ephemeral container?
questions.role: Temporary container added to running pod for debugging

questions.question: What is kubectl debug?
questions.role: Creates debug container to troubleshoot running pods/nodes

questions.question: What is K8s certificate rotation?
questions.role: Automatic renewal of TLS certificates used by control plane

questions.question: What is kubeadm?
questions.role: Tool for bootstrapping K8s clusters following best practices

questions.question: What is the purpose of node labels?
questions.role: Metadata for organizing nodes and controlling pod scheduling

questions.question: What does kubectl scale do?
questions.role: Changes number of replicas in Deployment/ReplicaSet/StatefulSet

questions.question: What is CI (Continuous Integration)?
questions.role: Automatically building and testing code on every commit

questions.question: What is CD (Continuous Delivery)?
questions.role: Automated process ensuring code is always in deployable state

questions.question: What is Continuous Deployment?
questions.role: Automatically deploying every passed build to production

questions.question: What is the difference between Continuous Delivery and Deployment?
questions.role: Delivery requires manual approval; Deployment is fully automatic

questions.question: What is Jenkins?
questions.role: Open-source automation server for building CI/CD pipelines

questions.question: What is a Jenkinsfile?
questions.role: Declarative or scripted pipeline definition as code in Git

questions.question: What are Jenkins stages?
questions.role: Logical divisions of a pipeline (Build, Test, Deploy)

questions.question: What is GitHub Actions?
questions.role: CI/CD platform integrated directly into GitHub repositories

questions.question: What is a workflow in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Automated process defined in .yml file triggered by events

questions.question: What is a job in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Set of steps running on the same runner

questions.question: What is a step in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Individual task (action or shell command) within a job

questions.question: What is a runner in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Server that executes the workflow jobs

questions.question: What is GitLab CI/CD?
questions.role: Built-in CI/CD platform in GitLab using .gitlab-ci.yml

questions.question: What is a pipeline in GitLab CI?
questions.role: Series of stages and jobs triggered on code changes

questions.question: What is CircleCI?
questions.role: Cloud-based CI/CD platform for fast builds

questions.question: What is Travis CI?
questions.role: Hosted CI service commonly used for open source projects

questions.question: What is Azure DevOps Pipelines?
questions.role: Microsoft's CI/CD service integrated with Azure cloud

questions.question: What is AWS CodePipeline?
questions.role: Amazon's CI/CD service for automating release pipelines

questions.question: What is artifact in CI/CD?
questions.role: Build output (binary, Docker image, JAR) produced by pipeline

questions.question: What is artifact registry?
questions.role: Repository storing build artifacts (e.g., JFrog Artifactory, Nexus)

questions.question: What is a build trigger?
questions.role: Event (push, PR, schedule) that starts a CI/CD pipeline

questions.question: What is branch protection?
questions.role: Rules preventing direct commits to protected branches (requires PR)

questions.question: What is a pull request (PR)?
questions.role: Proposal to merge changes from one branch to another with review

questions.question: What is code review in CI/CD?
questions.role: Manual inspection of code changes before merging to main branch

questions.question: What is a release pipeline vs build pipeline?
questions.role: Build creates artifact; release deploys it to environments

questions.question: What is environment in CI/CD?
questions.role: Deployment target: dev, staging, production

questions.question: What is a deployment gate?
questions.role: Condition that must pass before deploying to next environment

questions.question: What is blue-green deployment in CI/CD?
questions.role: Two identical production environments for zero-downtime releases

questions.question: What is canary release?
questions.role: Releasing to small % of users first to validate before full rollout

questions.question: What is a feature flag?
questions.role: Toggle enabling/disabling features without deploying new code

questions.question: What is the trunk-based development branching strategy?
questions.role: All developers commit to main (trunk) frequently, short-lived branches

questions.question: What is Git Flow?
questions.role: Branching strategy with main, develop, feature, release, hotfix branches

questions.question: What is a hotfix in CI/CD?
questions.role: Emergency fix deployed directly to production (bypassing normal process)

questions.question: What is a rollback strategy?
questions.role: Plan to revert to previous version if new deployment fails

questions.question: What is semantic versioning (SemVer)?
questions.role: Major.Minor.Patch version numbering (e.g., 2.1.3)

questions.question: What does MAJOR version increment mean in SemVer?
questions.role: Breaking API changes

questions.question: What does MINOR version increment mean in SemVer?
questions.role: New backward-compatible features

questions.question: What does PATCH version increment mean in SemVer?
questions.role: Backward-compatible bug fixes

questions.question: What is a monorepo?
questions.role: Single repository containing multiple projects/services

questions.question: What is a polyrepo?
questions.role: Separate repository per service/project

questions.question: What is DORA metrics?
questions.role: DevOps Research and Assessment: deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, change failure rate

questions.question: What is deployment frequency in DORA?
questions.role: How often code is deployed to production

questions.question: What is lead time for changes?
questions.role: Time from commit to running in production

questions.question: What is MTTR (Mean Time To Recovery)?
questions.role: Average time to recover from a production failure

questions.question: What is change failure rate?
questions.role: Percentage of deployments causing production failures

questions.question: What is a pipeline as code?
questions.role: Defining CI/CD pipeline in version-controlled files (Jenkinsfile, .yml)

questions.question: What is sonarqube?
questions.role: Static code analysis platform for code quality and security scanning

questions.question: What is SAST (Static Application Security Testing)?
questions.role: Analyzing source code for vulnerabilities without running it

questions.question: What is DAST (Dynamic Application Security Testing)?
questions.role: Testing running application for vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is dependency scanning in CI/CD?
questions.role: Checking third-party dependencies for known vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is a build cache in CI/CD?
questions.role: Storing build dependencies to speed up subsequent pipeline runs

questions.question: What is matrix testing?
questions.role: Running tests across multiple OS/language version combinations

questions.question: What is environment variable management in CI/CD?
questions.role: Securely storing and injecting secrets/config into pipelines

questions.question: What is a Docker-based CI agent?
questions.role: CI runner using Docker containers as ephemeral build environments

questions.question: What is GitOps in CI/CD context?
questions.role: Using Git as source of truth for deployment, automated reconciliation

questions.question: What is infrastructure as code testing?
questions.role: Validating IaC files (Terraform, CloudFormation) in CI pipeline

questions.question: What is shift-left testing?
questions.role: Moving testing earlier in development lifecycle to catch bugs sooner

questions.question: What is test-driven development (TDD)?
questions.role: Writing tests before writing the code they test

questions.question: What is behavior-driven development (BDD)?
questions.role: Using natural language (Gherkin) to describe tests from business perspective

questions.question: What is a nightly build?
questions.role: Automated build and test run scheduled each night

questions.question: What is parallel test execution?
questions.role: Running multiple test suites simultaneously to reduce total time

questions.question: What is a release candidate (RC)?
questions.role: Final pre-release version for testing before official release

questions.question: What is auto-scaling CI runners?
questions.role: Dynamically provisioning/removing runners based on job queue

questions.question: What is Tekton?
questions.role: Kubernetes-native CI/CD framework using CRDs

questions.question: What is ArgoCD's role in CI/CD?
questions.role: GitOps continuous delivery watching Git and syncing to K8s

questions.question: What is Spinnaker?
questions.role: Multi-cloud continuous delivery platform by Netflix

questions.question: What is an integration test environment?
questions.role: Dedicated environment where services are tested together

questions.question: What is smoke testing in CI/CD?
questions.role: Quick basic tests after deployment to verify critical functionality works

questions.question: What is the fail-fast principle in CI/CD?
questions.role: Run fastest, most likely to fail tests first to give quick feedback

questions.question: What is a deployment manifest?
questions.role: YAML/JSON file describing desired state of K8s resources

questions.question: What is infrastructure drift?
questions.role: Divergence between actual infrastructure and IaC definition

questions.question: What is immutable infrastructure?
questions.role: Infrastructure never modified — replaced with new instances on change

questions.question: What is GitLab's Auto DevOps?
questions.role: Auto-configuring CI/CD pipeline based on language and framework detection

questions.question: What is Dependabot?
questions.role: GitHub tool auto-creating PRs for outdated dependency updates

questions.question: What is a release gate/quality gate?
questions.role: Automated check ensuring code meets quality standards before progressing

questions.question: What is deployment validation?
questions.role: Automated checks after deployment to verify application health

questions.question: What is a self-hosted runner in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Runner deployed on your own infrastructure instead of GitHub's

questions.question: What is cache invalidation in CI/CD?
questions.role: Clearing build cache when dependencies change to ensure fresh builds

questions.question: What is environment parity?
questions.role: Keeping dev, staging, and production as identical as possible

questions.question: What is progressive delivery?
questions.role: Advanced deployment strategies (canary, feature flags) controlling rollout risk

questions.question: What is the purpose of code coverage gates?
questions.role: Block merges if code coverage drops below threshold

questions.question: What is Terraform?
questions.role: Infrastructure as Code tool for provisioning cloud resources declaratively

questions.question: What language does Terraform use?
questions.role: HCL (HashiCorp Configuration Language)

questions.question: What is a Terraform provider?
questions.role: Plugin enabling Terraform to interact with a specific API (AWS, GCP)

questions.question: What is a Terraform resource?
questions.role: Declares a specific infrastructure object to be created/managed

questions.question: What does terraform init do?
questions.role: Initializes working directory: downloads providers and modules

questions.question: What does terraform plan do?
questions.role: Shows what changes Terraform will make without applying them

questions.question: What does terraform apply do?
questions.role: Creates or modifies infrastructure according to plan

questions.question: What does terraform destroy do?
questions.role: Destroys all resources managed by current Terraform config

questions.question: What is Terraform state?
questions.role: File (terraform.tfstate) recording actual infrastructure state

questions.question: Why is Terraform state important?
questions.role: Allows Terraform to map config to real resources and detect drift

questions.question: What is remote state?
questions.role: Storing terraform.tfstate in remote backend (S3, Terraform Cloud)

questions.question: What is a Terraform backend?
questions.role: Where state is stored and operations are executed (S3, Azure Blob, etc.)

questions.question: What is state locking?
questions.role: Preventing concurrent state modifications (e.g., via DynamoDB lock)

questions.question: What is a Terraform module?
questions.role: Reusable container of Terraform resources for a logical component

questions.question: What is a Terraform variable?
questions.role: Input parameter for Terraform configuration (type, default, description)

questions.question: What is a Terraform output?
questions.role: Value exposed after apply, used by other modules or pipelines

questions.question: What is terraform.tfvars file?
questions.role: File containing variable values for a Terraform configuration

questions.question: What is the count meta-argument?
questions.role: Creates multiple instances of a resource

questions.question: What is the for_each meta-argument?
questions.role: Creates one resource instance per element in map/set

questions.question: What is the difference between count and for_each?
questions.role: for_each uses stable string keys; count uses numeric index

questions.question: What is a data source in Terraform?
questions.role: Fetches read-only information from existing resources or APIs

questions.question: What is terraform fmt?
questions.role: Formats Terraform files to canonical style

questions.question: What is terraform validate?
questions.role: Validates configuration syntax and internal consistency

questions.question: What is terraform import?
questions.role: Brings existing infrastructure under Terraform management

questions.question: What is terraform taint?
questions.role: Marks resource for destruction and recreation on next apply (deprecated)

questions.question: What replaced terraform taint?
questions.role: terraform apply -replace=resource.name

questions.question: What is terraform refresh?
questions.role: Updates state file to match real-world infrastructure

questions.question: What is the lifecycle meta-argument?
questions.role: Controls behavior: create_before_destroy, prevent_destroy, ignore_changes

questions.question: What does create_before_destroy do?
questions.role: Creates replacement before destroying old resource

questions.question: What does prevent_destroy do?
questions.role: Prevents terraform destroy from deleting specific resource

questions.question: What does ignore_changes do?
questions.role: Ignores specified attribute changes from drifting state

questions.question: What is a workspace in Terraform?
questions.role: Separate state instances for same configuration (dev, prod)

questions.question: What is the default workspace?
questions.role: 'default' workspace created automatically in Terraform

questions.question: What is Terraform Cloud?
questions.role: HashiCorp's managed service for Terraform collaboration and remote runs

questions.question: What is Sentinel?
questions.role: HashiCorp's policy-as-code framework for governance in Terraform Enterprise

questions.question: What is module registry?
questions.role: Centralized repository of reusable Terraform modules

questions.question: What is a provider lock file?
questions.role: terraform.lock.hcl — pins provider versions for reproducibility

questions.question: What is dynamic block in Terraform?
questions.role: Generates nested configuration blocks programmatically

questions.question: What is locals in Terraform?
questions.role: Named values computed from expressions, used like variables

questions.question: What is depends_on meta-argument?
questions.role: Explicitly declares dependency between resources when implicit fails

questions.question: What is the provisioner in Terraform?
questions.role: Executes scripts on local or remote resource after creation (use sparingly)

questions.question: What is user_data in cloud resources (Terraform)?
questions.role: Script run on VM first boot (cloud-init)

questions.question: What is terraform show?
questions.role: Displays human-readable output of state or plan file

questions.question: What is terraform state list?
questions.role: Lists resources in current state file

questions.question: What is terraform state show?
questions.role: Shows detailed attributes of specific resource in state

questions.question: What is terraform state rm?
questions.role: Removes resource from state without destroying it (for import elsewhere)

questions.question: What is terraform state mv?
questions.role: Moves resource in state (renaming or moving to module)

questions.question: What is the purpose of terraform output?
questions.role: Prints values of outputs from root module or sub-module

questions.question: What is version constraint in required_providers?
questions.role: Locks provider to compatible version range

questions.question: What is a null_resource?
questions.role: Resource with no specific provider action, used to run provisioners

questions.question: What is terraform console?
questions.role: Interactive REPL for evaluating Terraform expressions

questions.question: What is the difference between resource and data source?
questions.role: Resource creates/manages; data source reads existing without managing

questions.role: Applies changes to only specified resource(s)

questions.question: What is -var flag in terraform apply?
questions.role: Passes variable value from command line

questions.role: = assigns value; == is for comparison in conditions

questions.question: What is a template_file data source?
questions.role: Renders a template with variable substitutions (older pattern)

questions.question: What is templatefile() function?
questions.role: Built-in function replacing template_file, renders template with vars

questions.question: What is for expression in Terraform?
questions.role: Transforms collections using comprehension-like syntax

questions.question: What is conditional expression in Terraform?
questions.role: condition ? true_val : false_val — ternary operator

questions.question: What is splat expression (*)?
questions.role: Shorthand to get attribute from all elements in list

questions.question: What is a moved block in Terraform?
questions.role: Allows renaming/moving resources without destroy/recreate

questions.question: What is check block in Terraform 1.5+?
questions.role: Custom validation condition checked after apply to assert expected state

questions.question: What is precondition/postcondition?
questions.role: Assertions on resource inputs/outputs that must be true

questions.question: What is Terragrunt?
questions.role: Thin wrapper around Terraform for DRY configurations and remote state

questions.question: What is Pulumi vs Terraform?
questions.role: Pulumi uses real programming languages; Terraform uses HCL

questions.role: Write Terraform configs in TypeScript/Python using AWS CDK-style API

questions.question: What is resource drift in Terraform?
questions.role: Real infrastructure differs from Terraform state

questions.question: What does terraform providers do?
questions.role: Lists providers required by current configuration

questions.question: What is the purpose of .terraform directory?
questions.role: Stores downloaded providers and modules (auto-created by init)

questions.question: What is terraformer?
questions.role: Tool reverse-engineering existing infrastructure to Terraform code

questions.question: What is the purpose of required_version in Terraform?
questions.role: Constrains which Terraform CLI version can use the configuration

questions.question: What is a partial backend configuration?
questions.role: Specifying only some backend settings in config, rest from CLI/env

questions.question: What is terraform force-unlock?
questions.role: Manually releases state lock if automatic unlock failed

questions.question: What is the importance of storing state in S3 with versioning?
questions.role: Allows recovery from accidental state corruption or deletion

questions.question: What is a Terraform registry?
questions.role: Public/private repository for sharing modules and providers

questions.question: What is the purpose of tags in cloud resources via Terraform?
questions.role: Metadata for cost allocation, filtering, and governance

questions.question: What is cloud-agnostic infrastructure code?
questions.role: IaC working across multiple cloud providers

questions.question: What is the depends_on use case for modules?
questions.role: When module has hidden dependencies not captured by resource references

questions.question: What are provider aliases used for?
questions.role: Managing multiple instances of same provider (e.g., multiple AWS regions)

questions.question: What is the replace_triggered_by lifecycle argument?
questions.role: Forces resource replacement when another resource changes

questions.question: What is observability?
questions.role: Ability to understand internal state from external outputs (metrics, logs, traces)

questions.question: What are the three pillars of observability?
questions.role: Metrics, Logs, Traces

questions.question: What is Prometheus?
questions.role: Open-source monitoring system scraping and storing time-series metrics

questions.question: What is the Prometheus data model?
questions.role: Time-series identified by metric name and label key-value pairs

questions.question: What is PromQL?
questions.role: Prometheus Query Language for querying time-series data

questions.question: What is a Prometheus exporter?
questions.role: Exposes metrics in Prometheus format from external systems

questions.question: What is Grafana?
questions.role: Visualization platform for metrics, logs, and traces from multiple sources

questions.question: What is Grafana dashboard?
questions.role: Collection of panels visualizing metrics and data from data sources

questions.question: What is Alertmanager in Prometheus?
questions.role: Handles alert routing, grouping, silencing, and notification

questions.question: What is a Prometheus alert rule?
questions.role: Condition that fires when PromQL expression is true for duration

questions.question: What is the Loki stack?
questions.role: Grafana Loki for log aggregation, Promtail for collection

questions.question: What is ELK stack?
questions.role: Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana for log management

questions.question: What is Elasticsearch?
questions.role: Distributed search and analytics engine for log/data storage

questions.question: What is Logstash?
questions.role: Data processing pipeline for collecting, transforming, and sending logs

questions.question: What is Kibana?
questions.role: Visualization and exploration interface for Elasticsearch data

questions.question: What is Fluentd/Fluent Bit?
questions.role: Log collectors/forwarders aggregating logs from multiple sources

questions.question: What is distributed tracing?
questions.role: Tracking request as it travels through multiple microservices

questions.question: What is Jaeger?
questions.role: Open-source distributed tracing platform (CNCF)

questions.question: What is Zipkin?
questions.role: Distributed tracing system originally by Twitter

questions.question: What is OpenTelemetry?
questions.role: Vendor-neutral standard for collecting metrics, logs, and traces

questions.question: What is a span in distributed tracing?
questions.role: Single unit of work representing one operation in a trace

questions.question: What is a trace in distributed tracing?
questions.role: Complete journey of a request through all services

questions.question: What is trace context propagation?
questions.role: Passing trace ID across service boundaries for correlation

questions.question: What is SLI (Service Level Indicator)?
questions.role: Metric measuring a specific aspect of service performance

questions.question: What is SLO (Service Level Objective)?
questions.role: Target value for an SLI (e.g., 99.9% uptime)

questions.question: What is SLA (Service Level Agreement)?
questions.role: Contractual commitment to customers about service levels

questions.question: What is error budget?
questions.role: Amount of downtime/errors allowed before SLO is breached

questions.question: What is RED method of monitoring?
questions.role: Rate, Errors, Duration — key metrics for services

questions.question: What is USE method of monitoring?
questions.role: Utilization, Saturation, Errors — for infrastructure resources

questions.question: What is the Four Golden Signals?
questions.role: Latency, Traffic, Errors, Saturation (Google's monitoring approach)

questions.question: What is latency in monitoring?
questions.role: Time to serve a request (p50, p95, p99 percentiles)

questions.question: What is saturation in monitoring?
questions.role: How full a resource is (CPU, memory, queue depth)

questions.question: What is a metric cardinality problem?
questions.role: Too many unique label combinations causing memory explosion in Prometheus

questions.question: What is a scrape interval in Prometheus?
questions.role: Frequency at which Prometheus collects metrics from targets

questions.question: What is Prometheus pushgateway?
questions.role: Intermediary for pushing metrics from short-lived jobs

questions.question: What is a counter metric type?
questions.role: Monotonically increasing value (resets on restart) — e.g., total requests

questions.question: What is a gauge metric type?
questions.role: Value that can go up and down — e.g., current memory usage

questions.question: What is a histogram metric type?
questions.role: Samples observations in configurable buckets (e.g., request duration)

questions.question: What is a summary metric type?
questions.role: Calculates configurable quantiles over a sliding time window

questions.question: What is health check endpoint?
questions.role: API endpoint (/health, /ready) reporting application health status

questions.question: What is the difference between /health and /ready?
questions.role: Health checks if app is alive; ready checks if ready to serve traffic

questions.question: What is on-call rotation?
questions.role: Scheduling team members to be responsible for incident response

questions.question: What is incident management?
questions.role: Process for detecting, responding to, and resolving production issues

questions.question: What is MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures)?
questions.role: Average time between system failures

questions.question: What is postmortem (incident retrospective)?
questions.role: Blameless analysis of incident cause and prevention measures

questions.question: What is synthetic monitoring?
questions.role: Simulating user interactions to proactively test application availability

questions.question: What is Real User Monitoring (RUM)?
questions.role: Collecting performance data from actual user browsers

questions.question: What is APM (Application Performance Monitoring)?
questions.role: Monitoring application behavior and performance in production

questions.question: What is New Relic?
questions.role: Full-stack APM and observability platform

questions.question: What is Datadog?
questions.role: Cloud monitoring and analytics platform with APM, logs, metrics

questions.question: What is Dynatrace?
questions.role: AI-powered full-stack observability and monitoring platform

questions.question: What is CloudWatch?
questions.role: AWS native monitoring service for AWS resources and applications

questions.question: What is Azure Monitor?
questions.role: Microsoft's monitoring service for Azure resources

questions.question: What is Google Cloud Monitoring?
questions.role: GCP's native monitoring service (formerly Stackdriver)

questions.question: What is PagerDuty?
questions.role: Incident management and on-call scheduling platform

questions.question: What is VictorOps (Splunk On-Call)?
questions.role: Incident management and on-call scheduling platform

questions.question: What is the purpose of runbooks?
questions.role: Step-by-step procedures for handling known operational issues

questions.question: What is log level?
questions.role: Severity classification: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL

questions.question: What is structured logging?
questions.role: Logs in machine-parseable format (JSON) with consistent fields

questions.question: What is centralized logging?
questions.role: Collecting all application logs in one system for search and analysis

questions.question: What is log retention policy?
questions.role: Rules defining how long logs are stored before deletion or archival

questions.question: What is a correlation ID?
questions.role: Unique ID propagated across services to correlate logs for one request

questions.question: What is the difference between monitoring and observability?
questions.role: Monitoring checks known failures; observability enables understanding unknown issues

questions.question: What is the cardinality of labels in Prometheus?
questions.role: Number of unique label value combinations — high cardinality is expensive

questions.question: What is recording rule in Prometheus?
questions.role: Pre-computes expensive PromQL queries into new metrics for speed

questions.question: What is the Thanos project?
questions.role: Extends Prometheus for long-term storage and global querying

questions.question: What is Cortex?
questions.role: Horizontally scalable, multi-tenant, long-term Prometheus storage

questions.question: What is Mimir?
questions.role: Grafana's horizontally scalable Prometheus backend

questions.question: What is Tempo (Grafana)?
questions.role: Grafana's open-source distributed tracing backend

questions.role: Labels route alerts to specific receivers in Alertmanager

questions.question: What is inhibition rule in Alertmanager?
questions.role: Suppresses certain alerts when another related alert is firing

questions.question: What is silencing in Alertmanager?
questions.role: Muting alerts for a time period (e.g., during planned maintenance)

questions.question: What is anomaly detection in monitoring?
questions.role: Automatically identifying unusual patterns in metrics

questions.question: What is canary monitoring?
questions.role: Monitoring a small % deployment before full rollout

questions.question: What is capacity planning?
questions.role: Forecasting and provisioning resources before they hit limits

questions.question: What is SLO burn rate alert?
questions.role: Alert when error budget is being consumed too fast

questions.question: What is multi-window multi-burn-rate alert?
questions.role: Advanced SLO alerting detecting fast and slow error budget burns

questions.question: What is kube-state-metrics?

questions.question: What is Prometheus federation?
