questions.role: Alert that fires when a monitoring system stops working

questions.question: What is chaos engineering?
questions.role: Intentionally introducing failures to test system resilience

questions.question: What is Chaos Monkey?
questions.role: Netflix tool randomly terminating instances to test resilience

questions.question: What is the purpose of alerting fatigue prevention?
questions.role: Reducing noisy, low-signal alerts to prevent team ignoring real issues

questions.question: What is Git?
questions.role: Distributed version control system tracking code changes

questions.question: What does git init do?
questions.role: Initializes a new Git repository in current directory

questions.question: What does git clone do?
questions.role: Creates a local copy of a remote repository

questions.question: What does git add do?
questions.role: Stages changes for next commit

questions.question: What does git commit do?
questions.role: Records staged changes in repository history

questions.question: What does git push do?
questions.role: Uploads local commits to remote repository

questions.question: What does git pull do?
questions.role: Fetches remote changes and merges into current branch

questions.question: What is the difference between git fetch and git pull?
questions.role: Fetch downloads without merging; pull downloads and merges

questions.question: What does git status do?
questions.role: Shows working tree status (staged, unstaged, untracked files)

questions.question: What does git log do?
questions.role: Shows commit history

questions.question: What does git diff do?
questions.role: Shows differences between working tree and last commit

questions.question: What does git branch do?
questions.role: Lists, creates, or deletes branches

questions.question: What does git checkout do?
questions.role: Switches branches or restores working tree files

questions.question: What does git switch do?
questions.role: Modern replacement for git checkout for switching branches

questions.question: What does git merge do?
questions.role: Integrates changes from another branch into current branch

questions.question: What is the difference between merge and rebase?
questions.role: Merge creates merge commit; rebase rewrites history for linear log

questions.question: What does git rebase do?
questions.role: Moves/combines commits by reapplying them on top of another branch

questions.question: What is a merge conflict?
questions.role: Occurs when two branches change same part of same file differently

questions.question: How do you resolve a merge conflict?
questions.role: Edit conflicted file, remove markers, git add, git commit

questions.question: What does git stash do?
questions.role: Temporarily saves uncommitted changes to a clean working directory

questions.question: What does git stash pop do?
questions.role: Restores last stashed changes and removes from stash

questions.question: What does git reset do?
questions.role: Moves HEAD and branch pointer to specified commit

questions.question: What is the difference between git reset --soft, --mixed, --hard?
questions.role: soft: keeps staged+working; mixed: keeps working only; hard: discards all

questions.question: What does git revert do?
questions.role: Creates new commit undoing changes from a previous commit (non-destructive)

questions.question: What is the difference between git reset and git revert?
questions.role: Reset moves HEAD (destructive); revert creates new commit (safe)

questions.question: What does git cherry-pick do?
questions.role: Applies a specific commit from another branch to current branch

questions.question: What does git tag do?
questions.role: Creates a named reference to a specific commit (releases)

questions.question: What is an annotated tag vs lightweight tag?
questions.role: Annotated: full object with message; lightweight: just a pointer

questions.question: What is HEAD in Git?
questions.role: Pointer to currently checked-out commit or branch

questions.question: What is detached HEAD state?
questions.role: HEAD points directly to commit, not a branch

questions.question: What does git remote do?
questions.role: Manages connections to remote repositories

questions.question: What does git remote add do?
questions.role: Adds a new remote repository connection

questions.question: What is origin?
questions.role: Default name for primary remote repository

questions.question: What is upstream?
questions.role: Remote repository you forked from (or main remote)

questions.question: What does git bisect do?
questions.role: Binary search through commits to find which introduced a bug

questions.question: What does git blame do?
questions.role: Shows who changed each line of a file and when

questions.question: What is .gitignore?
questions.role: File listing patterns of files Git should not track

questions.question: What is a Git hook?
questions.role: Script triggered by Git events (pre-commit, post-push)

questions.question: What is a pre-commit hook?
questions.role: Script running before each commit (e.g., linting, tests)

questions.question: What is the staging area (index)?
questions.role: Intermediate area where changes are prepared before committing

questions.question: What does git add -p do?
questions.role: Interactively stages specific chunks of changes (patch mode)

questions.question: What is git commit --amend?
questions.role: Modifies the last commit (message or files)

questions.question: What does git reflog do?
questions.role: Shows history of all HEAD changes, including resets and checkouts

questions.question: Why is git reflog useful?
questions.role: Can recover lost commits after accidental reset or branch deletion

questions.question: What is a squash merge?
questions.role: Combines all commits from a branch into one before merging

questions.question: What is fast-forward merge?
questions.role: Moves branch pointer forward without creating merge commit (linear history)

questions.question: What is the --no-ff flag?
questions.role: Forces merge commit even when fast-forward is possible

questions.question: What is an interactive rebase?
questions.role: git rebase -i — allows squash, reword, drop, reorder commits

questions.question: What is squashing commits?
questions.role: Combining multiple commits into one for cleaner history

questions.question: What is the GitHub Flow branching model?
questions.role: main + short-lived feature branches + PR to merge

questions.question: What is the purpose of git pull --rebase?
questions.role: Pulls remote changes and rebases local commits instead of merge commit

questions.question: What is a shallow clone?
questions.role: Clones only limited commit history (--depth N) for faster download

questions.question: What is git submodule?
questions.role: Embeds another repository inside a parent repository at a specific commit

questions.question: What does git worktree do?
questions.role: Allows multiple working directories from same repository

questions.question: What is git archive?
questions.role: Creates archive (zip/tar) of repository content

questions.question: What is git shortlog?
questions.role: Summarizes git log output by author

questions.question: What is git grep?
questions.role: Searches for patterns in tracked files

questions.question: What does ORIG_HEAD contain after a merge?
questions.role: Reference to previous HEAD before the merge

questions.question: What is FETCH_HEAD?
questions.role: Latest fetched commit from last git fetch

questions.question: What is MERGE_HEAD?
questions.role: Commit being merged in a currently active merge

questions.question: What is git gc?
questions.role: Garbage collection — cleans up unnecessary files and optimizes repo

questions.question: What is git prune?
questions.role: Removes objects unreachable from any reference

questions.question: What is git remote prune origin?
questions.role: Removes stale remote-tracking branches deleted on remote

questions.question: What is a bare repository?
questions.role: Repository without working tree, used as remote/server

questions.question: What is git bundle?
questions.role: Creates single file containing repository data for offline transfer

questions.question: What does git clean do?
questions.role: Removes untracked files from working directory

questions.question: What is the difference between tracked and untracked files?
questions.role: Tracked files are known to Git; untracked are new, not yet added

questions.question: What is modified vs staged vs committed?
questions.role: Modified: changed but not staged; staged: ready to commit; committed: in history

questions.question: What does git log --oneline do?
questions.role: Shows condensed one-line format for each commit

questions.question: What does git log --graph do?
questions.role: Shows ASCII graph of branch and merge history

questions.question: What is git config --global?
questions.role: Sets configuration for all repositories for current user

questions.question: What is the purpose of commit signing?
questions.role: Verifies commit was made by specific person using GPG key

questions.question: What is git rerere?
questions.role: Reuse Recorded Resolution — auto-applies previous conflict resolutions

questions.question: What is the rule about rewriting public history?
questions.role: Never rebase or force-push commits already shared with others

questions.question: What is force push (git push -f)?
questions.role: Overwrites remote history (dangerous for shared branches)

questions.question: What is git push --force-with-lease?
questions.role: Safer force push that fails if remote was updated since last fetch

questions.question: What is conventional commits?
questions.role: Structured commit message format (feat:, fix:, chore:, etc.)

questions.question: What is semantic-release?
questions.role: Automates versioning and changelog based on conventional commits

questions.question: What is git-flow tool?
questions.role: CLI implementing the Git Flow branching strategy

questions.question: What is the purpose of PR (Pull Request) templates?
questions.role: Standardize PR descriptions for consistency and completeness

questions.question: What is CODEOWNERS file?
questions.role: Defines who must review changes to specific files/directories

questions.question: What does Signed-off-by mean in commits?
questions.role: Developer certifies they have rights to contribute the code (DCO)

questions.question: What is the DCO (Developer Certificate of Origin)?
questions.role: Certification that contribution is original work (used by Linux kernel et al.)

questions.question: What is Large File Storage (LFS) in Git?
questions.role: Extension for versioning large files outside main repository

questions.question: What does git ls-files do?
questions.role: Lists all tracked files in the repository

questions.question: What is the sparse-checkout feature?
questions.role: Checks out only specific files/directories from large repos

questions.question: What is the purpose of git notes?
questions.role: Attach additional text to commits without changing commit hash

questions.question: What is a DevOps pipeline?
questions.role: Automated sequence of steps from code commit to production deployment

questions.question: What are the typical stages of a CI/CD pipeline?
questions.role: Source → Build → Test → Deploy → Monitor

questions.question: What is a pipeline trigger?
questions.role: Event (commit, tag, PR, schedule) that starts a pipeline run

questions.question: What is a webhook?
questions.role: HTTP callback notifying external systems about events (code pushes)

questions.question: What is a build artifact?

questions.question: What is artifact versioning?
questions.role: Tagging build outputs with version numbers for traceability

questions.question: What is a multi-stage pipeline?
questions.role: Pipeline with distinct phases: build, unit test, integration test, deploy

questions.role: Declarative uses structured syntax; Scripted uses Groovy code

questions.question: What is a parallel stage?
questions.role: Multiple steps running simultaneously to reduce total pipeline time

questions.question: What is a pipeline stage gate?
questions.role: Manual approval required before proceeding to next stage

questions.question: What is environment promotion?
questions.role: Moving artifact through dev → staging → production environments

questions.question: What is the difference between a build server and an agent?
questions.role: Build server coordinates; agent executes jobs

questions.question: What is a shared library in Jenkins?
questions.role: Reusable Groovy code shared across multiple pipelines

questions.question: What is a pipeline template?
questions.role: Pre-built pipeline definition that teams can reuse/extend

questions.question: What is a self-service pipeline?
questions.role: Developers create/configure their own pipelines without ops team

questions.question: What is the build context in Docker within CI?

questions.question: What is layer caching in Docker CI builds?
questions.role: Reusing unchanged Docker layers to speed up builds

questions.question: What is kaniko?
questions.role: Tool for building Docker images inside Kubernetes without Docker daemon

questions.question: What is buildah?
questions.role: Tool building OCI images without Docker daemon (rootless)

questions.question: What is a pipeline graph?
questions.role: Visual representation of pipeline stages and dependencies

questions.question: What is pipeline observability?
questions.role: Monitoring pipeline health, duration, failure rate, and bottlenecks

questions.question: What is flaky test?
questions.role: Test that sometimes passes and sometimes fails without code changes

questions.question: What is test quarantine?
questions.role: Isolating flaky tests to prevent blocking pipelines while fixing

questions.question: What is a monorepo pipeline challenge?
questions.role: Determining which services to build/test based on changed files

questions.question: What is affected testing in monorepos?
questions.role: Running tests only for packages affected by changed files

questions.question: What is pipeline caching?
questions.role: Storing dependencies between runs to avoid re-downloading

questions.question: What is a Docker layer cache mount in BuildKit?
questions.role: Mounts build cache directory as Docker layer during build

questions.question: What is secret management in pipelines?
questions.role: Securely storing and injecting credentials into pipeline steps

questions.question: What tools are used for secret management in CI/CD?
questions.role: HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault

questions.question: What is code coverage in CI?
questions.role: Percentage of code executed by automated tests

questions.question: What is the quality gate in CI?
questions.role: Condition blocking merge/deploy if code quality metrics fail threshold

questions.question: What is lint checking in CI?
questions.role: Automated code style and error analysis

questions.question: What is static analysis in CI?
questions.role: Analyzing code without executing it for bugs and quality issues

questions.question: What is OWASP dependency check?
questions.role: Scans dependencies for known CVEs

questions.question: What is Snyk in CI/CD?
questions.role: Security platform scanning code and dependencies for vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is a deployment pipeline vs release pipeline?

questions.question: What is change management in CI/CD?
questions.role: Process ensuring changes are approved and recorded before deployment

questions.question: What is ChatOps?
questions.role: Using chat tools (Slack) to trigger and monitor deployments

questions.question: What is the pipeline success rate metric?
questions.role: Percentage of pipeline runs that complete successfully

questions.question: What is mean time to pipeline completion?
questions.role: Average duration from trigger to finished pipeline run

questions.question: What is pipeline idempotency?
questions.role: Running pipeline multiple times produces same result

questions.question: What is a pipeline cleanup step?
questions.role: Removes temporary files, containers, and resources after run

questions.question: What is retry mechanism in pipelines?

questions.question: What is timeout in pipeline steps?
questions.role: Maximum time a step can run before being killed

questions.role: Fan-out: parallel jobs; Fan-in: waits for all to complete before proceeding

questions.question: What is a workspace in CI/CD pipelines?
questions.role: Shared storage for artifacts between pipeline stages/jobs

questions.question: What is pipeline migration?
questions.role: Moving pipeline definitions from one CI/CD platform to another

questions.question: What is the role of environment variables in pipelines?
questions.role: Pass configuration, credentials, and feature flags to pipeline steps

questions.question: What is a composite action in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Reusable action combining multiple steps into one callable unit

questions.question: What is a reusable workflow in GitHub Actions?
questions.role: Entire workflow called from another workflow

questions.question: What is path filtering in CI triggers?
questions.role: Run pipeline only when specific file paths change

questions.question: What is scheduled pipeline?
questions.role: Pipeline triggered at specific time intervals (cron)

questions.question: What is a smoke test in pipeline?
questions.role: Quick test after deployment verifying critical paths work

questions.question: What is pipeline as product?
questions.role: Treating CI/CD pipeline as a first-class product with SLAs and ownership

questions.question: What is infrastructure pipeline?

questions.question: What is GitOps pipeline?

questions.question: What is a deployment token?

questions.question: What is pipeline YAML anchors?

questions.question: What is matrix in GitHub Actions?

questions.question: What is a self-healing pipeline?

questions.question: What is a golden path pipeline?

questions.question: What is deployment ring in pipelines?

questions.question: What is the purpose of the build manifest?
questions.role: Records exactly what went into a build for reproducibility

questions.question: What is SBOM (Software Bill of Materials)?
questions.role: List of all open-source components and dependencies in software

questions.question: What is provenance attestation in CI/CD?
questions.role: Cryptographically signed record of how an artifact was built

questions.question: What is SLSA framework?
questions.role: Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts — security framework for build integrity

questions.question: What is sigstore?
questions.role: Open standard for signing software artifacts and verifying their provenance

questions.question: What is cosign?
questions.role: Tool from sigstore for signing and verifying container images

questions.question: What is pipeline dependency graph?
questions.role: DAG showing order and dependencies of all pipeline steps/jobs

questions.question: What is long-running test handling?
questions.role: Timeouts, parallelism, and test selection to manage slow tests

questions.question: What is test impact analysis?
questions.role: Determining which tests are affected by code changes to run selectively

questions.question: What is binary authorization?
questions.role: Policy requiring builds to be attested before deployment to GKE

questions.question: What is the security pipeline concept?
questions.role: Integrating security scanning into every stage of CI/CD pipeline

questions.question: What is shift-right testing?
questions.role: Testing in production with monitoring, chaos engineering, A/B tests

questions.question: What is a canary analysis pipeline?
questions.role: Automated metrics comparison between canary and baseline deployments

questions.question: What is the purpose of build numbering?
questions.role: Unique identifier for each build for traceability and rollback

questions.question: What is the pipeline contract?
questions.role: Agreement between pipeline and teams on inputs, outputs, and SLAs

questions.question: What is supply chain security in CI/CD?
questions.role: Protecting the integrity of code, build, and deployment processes

questions.question: What is hermetic build?
questions.role: Build that runs without network access, using only declared dependencies

questions.question: What is reproducible build?
questions.role: Rebuilding same source always produces bit-for-bit identical artifact

questions.question: What is the purpose of checksums in artifact distribution?
questions.role: Verify artifact integrity and detect tampering during download

questions.question: What is a pipeline health dashboard?
questions.role: Real-time view of pipeline status, success rate, and queue depth

questions.question: What is DevSecOps?
questions.role: Integrating security practices into DevOps pipeline from the start

questions.question: What is the principle of least privilege?
questions.role: Grant only minimum permissions needed for a task

questions.question: What is zero trust security?
questions.role: Never trust, always verify — validate every request regardless of origin

questions.question: What is a CVE?
questions.role: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures — identifier for security vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is a CVSS score?
questions.role: Numeric score 0-10 rating severity of a vulnerability

questions.question: What is dependency confusion attack?
questions.role: Attacker publishes malicious package with same name as internal package

questions.question: What is supply chain attack?
questions.role: Compromising software during build/distribution rather than at target directly

questions.question: What is container image hardening?
questions.role: Minimizing image surface area: non-root user, no unnecessary tools

questions.question: What is Pod Security Admission in K8s?
questions.role: Built-in admission controller enforcing security standards for pods

questions.question: What is network segmentation?
questions.role: Dividing network into isolated segments to limit breach impact

questions.question: What is TLS (Transport Layer Security)?
questions.role: Cryptographic protocol for secure communication over networks

questions.question: What is mutual TLS (mTLS)?
questions.role: Both client and server authenticate each other with certificates

questions.question: What is a service mesh security benefit?
questions.role: Automatic mTLS between all service-to-service communications

questions.question: What is secrets rotation?
questions.role: Periodically changing passwords and keys to limit exposure window

questions.question: What is HashiCorp Vault?
questions.role: Secrets management platform with dynamic secrets and encryption

questions.question: What is dynamic secret?
questions.role: Short-lived, auto-generated credential created on demand by Vault

questions.question: What is RBAC in cloud security?
questions.role: Role-Based Access Control assigning permissions through roles

questions.question: What is IAM (Identity and Access Management)?
questions.role: Service managing who has access to what cloud resources

questions.question: What is the difference between authentication and authorization?
questions.role: Authentication verifies identity; authorization determines permissions

questions.question: What is MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication)?
questions.role: Requiring two or more verification factors to access a system

questions.question: What is IAST (Interactive Application Security Testing)?
questions.role: Agent inside running app analyzing behavior during functional testing

questions.question: What is penetration testing?
questions.role: Authorized simulated attack to identify security vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is bug bounty program?
questions.role: Reward program for security researchers finding vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is OWASP Top 10?
questions.role: List of top 10 web application security risks

questions.question: What is SQL injection?
questions.role: Inserting malicious SQL into input to manipulate database queries

questions.question: What is XSS (Cross-Site Scripting)?
questions.role: Injecting malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users

questions.question: What is CSRF (Cross-Site Request Forgery)?
questions.role: Tricking authenticated users into executing unwanted actions

questions.question: What is SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)?
questions.role: Attacker causes server to make requests to internal/arbitrary URLs

questions.question: What is a WAF (Web Application Firewall)?
questions.role: Filters and monitors HTTP traffic to protect web applications

questions.question: What is rate limiting?
questions.role: Restricting number of requests per time period to prevent abuse

questions.question: What is DDoS protection?
questions.role: Defending against Distributed Denial of Service attacks flooding with traffic

questions.question: What is container escape?
questions.role: Attacker breaking out of container to access host system

questions.question: What is seccomp in container security?
questions.role: Linux kernel feature filtering system calls containers can make

questions.question: What is AppArmor?
questions.role: Linux security module providing mandatory access control for processes

questions.question: What is a security context in K8s?
questions.role: Defines privilege and access control settings for a Pod/container

questions.question: What is runAsNonRoot in K8s security?
questions.role: Ensures container runs as non-root user (best practice)

questions.question: What is allowPrivilegeEscalation: false?
questions.role: Prevents container process from gaining more privileges than parent

questions.question: What is readOnlyRootFilesystem?
questions.role: Makes container root filesystem read-only (limits attack surface)

questions.question: What is image signing?
questions.role: Cryptographically signing images to verify they haven't been tampered

questions.question: What is the principle of defense in depth?
questions.role: Multiple security layers so compromising one doesn't expose everything

questions.question: What is a security audit log?
questions.role: Immutable record of security-relevant events for investigation

questions.question: What is SIEM (Security Information and Event Management)?
questions.role: System collecting and analyzing security logs for threat detection

questions.question: What is SOC (Security Operations Center)?
questions.role: Team monitoring and responding to security incidents

questions.question: What is data encryption at rest?
questions.role: Encrypting stored data to protect against unauthorized access

questions.question: What is data encryption in transit?
questions.role: Encrypting data as it moves across networks (TLS/HTTPS)

questions.question: What is key management?
questions.role: Securely generating, storing, rotating, and deleting cryptographic keys

questions.question: What is PKI (Public Key Infrastructure)?
questions.role: System managing digital certificates and public key encryption

questions.question: What is a digital certificate?
questions.role: Electronic document binding a public key to an entity's identity

questions.question: What is certificate rotation?
questions.role: Replacing expiring certificates before they expire to prevent outages

questions.question: What is Let's Encrypt?
questions.role: Free, automated certificate authority for TLS certificates

questions.question: What is SSO (Single Sign-On)?
questions.role: One authentication granting access to multiple applications

questions.question: What is OAuth 2.0?
questions.role: Authorization framework allowing limited third-party access to resources

questions.question: What is OIDC (OpenID Connect)?
questions.role: Identity layer on top of OAuth 2.0 for authentication

questions.question: What is a JWT (JSON Web Token)?
questions.role: Compact, self-contained token for securely transmitting information

questions.question: What is JWT structure?
questions.role: Header.Payload.Signature — three base64url-encoded parts

questions.question: What is the problem with storing JWT in localStorage?
questions.role: Vulnerable to XSS — attacker can steal token with injected script

questions.question: What is SPIFFE/SPIRE?
questions.role: Standards for workload identity in cloud-native environments

questions.question: What is network policy in K8s security?
questions.role: Controls ingress/egress traffic between pods using label selectors

questions.question: What is baseline vs restricted pod security standard?
questions.role: Baseline: minimal restrictions; Restricted: follows current hardening best practices

questions.question: What is SBOM security benefit?
questions.role: Knowing exactly what's in software to quickly identify vulnerable components

questions.question: What is code signing in CI/CD?
questions.role: Attaching digital signature to code to verify authenticity and integrity

questions.question: What is vulnerability disclosure?
questions.role: Process for reporting and communicating security vulnerabilities responsibly

questions.question: What is the security principle of fail securely?
questions.role: On failure, default to secure state (deny access) rather than open

questions.question: What is security scanning in container registries?
questions.role: Automatic scanning of images on push for known vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is runtime security?
questions.role: Detecting and preventing threats in running containers and workloads

questions.question: What is Falco?
questions.role: Cloud-native runtime security tool detecting threats in containers/K8s

questions.question: What is Trivy?
questions.role: Vulnerability scanner for containers, filesystems, and repositories

questions.question: What is OIDC federation in CI/CD?
questions.role: Allowing CI/CD to authenticate to cloud using OIDC tokens (no long-lived secrets)

questions.question: What is the AWS IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts)?
questions.role: K8s service accounts assuming AWS IAM roles via OIDC federation

questions.question: What is Workload Identity in GCP?
questions.role: Kubernetes service accounts mapped to GCP IAM for secure cloud access

questions.question: What is security posture management?
questions.role: Continuously assessing and improving security configuration

questions.question: What is CSPM (Cloud Security Posture Management)?
questions.role: Identifying misconfiguration risks in cloud environments

questions.question: What is privilege escalation prevention?
questions.role: Blocking containers from gaining more privileges than at start

questions.question: What is image tag immutability?
questions.role: Preventing tags from being overwritten to ensure deployment integrity

questions.question: What is admission control?
questions.role: Intercepting K8s API requests to validate/mutate resources for security

questions.question: What is OPA (Open Policy Agent)?
questions.role: General-purpose policy engine for cloud-native authorization

questions.question: What is a security baseline?
questions.role: Minimum security requirements that all systems must meet

questions.question: What is threat modeling?
questions.role: Identifying potential threats and vulnerabilities in system design phase

questions.question: What is the STRIDE framework?
questions.role: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Info Disclosure, DoS, Elevation of Privilege

questions.question: What is compliance as code?
questions.role: Defining compliance requirements as automated policy checks in pipeline

questions.question: What is CIS Benchmarks?
questions.role: Security configuration guidelines for hardening systems and cloud services

questions.question: What is SOC 2 compliance?
questions.role: Trust services criteria for security, availability, confidentiality of cloud services

questions.question: What is GDPR impact on DevOps?
questions.role: Data privacy requirements affecting how personal data is stored/processed in pipelines

questions.question: What is the role of security champions?
questions.role: Developers embedded in teams who advocate for security practices

questions.question: What is red team vs blue team?
questions.role: Red team attacks; blue team defends to test security posture

questions.question: What is a purple team exercise?
questions.role: Red and blue teams collaborate to improve both attack and defense

questions.question: What is CVE scanning frequency best practice?
questions.role: Continuous scanning — on push, schedule, and triggered by new CVE releases

questions.question: What is credential scanning?
questions.role: Detecting secrets accidentally committed to source code repositories

questions.question: What is GitGuardian?
questions.role: Tool for detecting secrets and credentials exposed in Git repositories

questions.question: What is gitleaks?
questions.role: Open-source tool for detecting hardcoded secrets in Git repositories

questions.question: What is cloud computing?
questions.role: Delivering IT resources (compute, storage, networking) over the internet on demand

questions.question: What are the three cloud service models?
questions.role: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS

questions.question: What is IaaS?
questions.role: Infrastructure as a Service — virtual machines, storage, networking

questions.question: What is PaaS?
questions.role: Platform as a Service — managed runtime for deploying applications

questions.question: What is SaaS?
questions.role: Software as a Service — fully managed application (Gmail, Salesforce)

questions.question: What is FaaS?
questions.role: Function as a Service — serverless function execution (Lambda, Cloud Functions)

questions.question: What is serverless computing?
questions.role: Cloud executes code without managing server infrastructure

questions.question: What is AWS?
questions.role: Amazon's cloud platform — largest cloud provider

questions.question: What is Azure?
questions.role: Microsoft's cloud platform

questions.question: What is GCP (Google Cloud Platform)?
questions.role: Google's cloud infrastructure and services platform

questions.question: What is AWS EC2?
questions.role: Elastic Compute Cloud — virtual servers in AWS

questions.question: What is AWS S3?
questions.role: Simple Storage Service — object storage in AWS

questions.question: What is AWS Lambda?
questions.role: Serverless function service running code without managing servers

questions.question: What is AWS RDS?
questions.role: Relational Database Service — managed relational databases

questions.question: What is AWS VPC?
questions.role: Virtual Private Cloud — isolated network environment in AWS

questions.question: What is a cloud region?
questions.role: Geographic area containing multiple availability zones

questions.question: What is an availability zone?
questions.role: Isolated data centers within a region for fault tolerance

questions.question: What is multi-region deployment?
questions.role: Running applications across multiple geographic regions for resilience

questions.question: What is cloud elasticity?
questions.role: Automatically scaling resources up/down based on demand

questions.question: What is auto-scaling in cloud?
questions.role: Automatically adjusting number of compute resources based on load

questions.question: What is a load balancer?
questions.role: Distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers/instances

questions.question: What is AWS ELB?
questions.role: Elastic Load Balancer — distributes traffic across EC2 instances

questions.question: What is the difference between ALB and NLB in AWS?
questions.role: ALB works at HTTP layer (L7); NLB works at TCP layer (L4)

questions.question: What is AWS CloudFront?
questions.role: Content Delivery Network (CDN) for distributing content globally

questions.question: What is a CDN?
questions.role: Network of distributed servers delivering content from nearest location

questions.question: What is AWS IAM?
questions.role: Identity and Access Management — controls access to AWS resources

questions.question: What is an IAM role vs IAM user?
questions.role: Role is temporary credentials for services; user is permanent human identity

questions.question: What is an IAM policy?
questions.role: Document defining allowed/denied actions on AWS resources

questions.question: What is AWS CloudWatch?
questions.role: Monitoring and logging service for AWS resources

questions.question: What is AWS ECS?
questions.role: Elastic Container Service — managed Docker container orchestration

questions.question: What is AWS EKS?
questions.role: Elastic Kubernetes Service — managed Kubernetes on AWS

questions.question: What is AWS Fargate?
questions.role: Serverless compute engine for containers (ECS/EKS without managing nodes)

questions.question: What is AWS ECR?
questions.role: Elastic Container Registry — private Docker image registry in AWS

questions.question: What is Azure AKS?
questions.role: Azure Kubernetes Service — managed Kubernetes on Azure

questions.question: What is GKE?
questions.role: Google Kubernetes Engine — managed Kubernetes on GCP

questions.question: What is infrastructure as code in cloud?
questions.role: Provisioning cloud resources via code (Terraform, CloudFormation)

questions.question: What is AWS CloudFormation?
questions.role: AWS's native IaC service for provisioning AWS resources

questions.question: What is the Shared Responsibility Model?
questions.role: Cloud provider secures infrastructure; customer secures their data and apps

questions.question: What is cloud cost optimization?
questions.role: Strategies to reduce cloud spending (right-sizing, reserved instances)

questions.question: What are reserved instances in AWS?
questions.role: Committing to 1 or 3 year usage for significant discount vs on-demand

questions.question: What are spot instances?
questions.role: Spare AWS capacity at steep discount but can be interrupted

questions.question: What is AWS Savings Plans?
questions.role: Flexible pricing model with commitment to consistent usage for discount

questions.question: What is FinOps?
questions.role: Practice combining finance, operations, and engineering for cloud cost management

questions.question: What is multi-cloud strategy?
questions.role: Using services from multiple cloud providers to avoid lock-in

questions.question: What is hybrid cloud?
questions.role: Combining private (on-premise) and public cloud environments

questions.question: What is edge computing?
questions.role: Processing data near where it is generated rather than central cloud

questions.question: What is AWS Direct Connect?
questions.role: Dedicated private network connection between on-premise and AWS

questions.question: What is VPC peering?
questions.role: Direct network connection between two VPCs within same or different accounts

questions.question: What is Transit Gateway?
questions.role: Central hub connecting multiple VPCs and on-premise networks

questions.question: What is AWS Route 53?
questions.role: Scalable DNS and domain management service in AWS

questions.question: What is a NAT Gateway?
questions.role: Allows instances in private subnet to initiate outbound internet traffic

questions.question: What is an Internet Gateway?
questions.role: Connects VPC to the public internet

questions.question: What is a private subnet?
questions.role: Subnet with no direct internet access (uses NAT for outbound)

questions.question: What is a security group in AWS?
questions.role: Virtual firewall controlling inbound/outbound traffic for EC2 instances

questions.question: What is the difference between security group and NACL?
questions.role: Security groups are stateful (at instance level); NACLs are stateless (at subnet level)

questions.question: What is AWS SQS?
questions.role: Simple Queue Service — managed message queuing

questions.question: What is AWS SNS?
questions.role: Simple Notification Service — pub/sub messaging

questions.question: What is AWS EventBridge?
questions.role: Serverless event bus connecting AWS services and custom apps

questions.question: What is event-driven architecture?
questions.role: Services communicate via events/messages rather than direct calls

questions.question: What is AWS API Gateway?
questions.role: Managed service for creating, publishing, and securing REST/WebSocket APIs

questions.question: What is service mesh in cloud-native?
questions.role: Infrastructure layer handling service-to-service communication (Istio, Linkerd)

questions.question: What is cloud-native application?
questions.role: Application designed specifically for cloud environments (12-factor, microservices)

questions.question: What is the 12-factor app methodology?
questions.role: Best practices for building scalable, maintainable cloud-native apps

questions.question: What is factor III (Config) in 12-factor?
questions.role: Store configuration in environment variables, not code

questions.question: What is factor VI (Processes) in 12-factor?
questions.role: Execute app as stateless processes with no shared state

questions.question: What is a microservice architecture?
questions.role: Decomposing application into small, independently deployable services

questions.question: What is service discovery?
questions.role: How microservices find and connect to each other dynamically

questions.question: What is AWS service discovery (Cloud Map)?
questions.role: Managed service discovery for cloud resources and microservices

questions.question: What is circuit breaker pattern?
questions.role: Stops requests to failing service and fails fast to prevent cascade

questions.question: What is bulkhead pattern?
questions.role: Isolating resources for different services to prevent one failing all

questions.question: What is retry with exponential backoff?
questions.role: Retrying failed requests with increasing delay to reduce load

questions.question: What is eventual consistency?
questions.role: Distributed systems where all nodes will eventually have same data (not immediate)

questions.question: What is CAP theorem?
questions.role: Consistency, Availability, Partition Tolerance — can only guarantee 2 of 3

questions.question: What is a stateless service?
questions.role: Service that stores no session state and can be replaced by any instance

questions.question: What is object storage vs block storage?
questions.role: Object: flat namespace with metadata; Block: low-level raw storage

questions.question: What is AWS EBS?
questions.role: Elastic Block Store — persistent block storage for EC2 instances

questions.question: What is AWS EFS?
questions.role: Elastic File System — managed NFS for shared file access across EC2

questions.question: What is the purpose of AWS Elastic Beanstalk?
questions.role: PaaS for deploying web apps without managing underlying infrastructure

questions.question: What is AWS Lightsail?
questions.role: Simplified cloud service for small applications (VPS-like)

questions.question: What is infrastructure drift detection in cloud?
questions.role: Finding differences between IaC definition and actual cloud state

questions.question: What is cloud landing zone?
questions.role: Pre-configured environment with security, governance, and networking best practices

questions.question: What is AWS Organizations?
questions.role: Managing multiple AWS accounts with centralized policies and billing

questions.question: What is a service catalog in cloud?
questions.role: Pre-approved cloud resources/templates teams can deploy self-service

questions.question: What is cloud governance?
questions.role: Policies and controls ensuring cloud usage is compliant, cost-effective, secure

questions.question: What is CapEx vs OpEx in cloud?
questions.role: CapEx is upfront investment; OpEx is ongoing operational costs

questions.question: What is total cost of ownership (TCO) in cloud?
questions.role: Complete cost including compute, storage, network, support, and management

questions.question: What is cloud native CNCF?
questions.role: Cloud Native Computing Foundation — open standards and projects for cloud-native

questions.question: What is Kubernetes' role in the cloud-native ecosystem?
questions.role: Standard for container orchestration enabling cloud-portable deployments

questions.question: What is the difference between vertical and horizontal scaling?
questions.role: Vertical adds resources to one machine; horizontal adds more machines

questions.question: What is chaos engineering in cloud?
questions.role: Testing resilience by intentionally causing failures in production

questions.question: What is a Docker health check exit code?
questions.role: 0=healthy, 1=unhealthy — used to trigger container restart policies

questions.question: What is the HEALTHCHECK interval?
questions.role: Time between health check runs (default 30s)

questions.question: What is the HEALTHCHECK retries?
questions.role: Consecutive failures before container is marked unhealthy (default 3)

questions.question: What is the HEALTHCHECK start-period?
questions.role: Grace period during startup before health check failures count (default 0s)

questions.question: What is docker system df?
questions.role: Shows Docker disk usage by images, containers, and volumes

questions.question: What is docker system prune?
questions.role: Removes all stopped containers, dangling images, unused networks/build cache

questions.question: What is the --volumes flag on docker system prune?
questions.role: Also removes unused volumes (dangerous — data loss possible)

questions.question: What is docker image prune -a?
questions.role: Removes all unused images not just dangling ones

questions.question: What is a dangling image?
questions.role: Image with no tag and not referenced by any container

questions.question: What is Docker BuildKit inline cache?
questions.role: --cache-from with --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 for CI caching

questions.question: What is the GitHub Actions Docker layer cache?
questions.role: actions/cache caching Docker layers between CI runs

questions.question: What is Docker's --target flag in multistage build?
questions.role: Build only up to specific stage: docker build --target builder

questions.question: What is the Docker context server?
questions.role: Docker daemon managing images/containers — can be remote via TCP/SSH

questions.question: What is docker context?
questions.role: Named Docker endpoint configuration (local or remote daemon)

questions.question: What is docker context use?
questions.role: Switches active Docker context to target daemon

questions.question: What is the DOCKER_HOST variable?
questions.role: Overrides Docker daemon socket/address for client connections

questions.question: What is docker scout?
questions.role: Docker's vulnerability scanning and software composition analysis tool

questions.question: What is Docker's rootless mode?
questions.role: Running Docker daemon as non-root user for improved security

questions.question: What is Docker in Docker security risk?
questions.role: Requires privileged mode or host socket mount — security vulnerabilities

questions.question: What is kaniko as dind alternative?
questions.role: Builds Docker images without Docker daemon — safe in Kubernetes

questions.question: What is kubelet?
questions.role: Agent on each node ensuring containers are running in Pods

questions.question: What is kube-proxy's role?
questions.role: Maintains iptables/IPVS rules on nodes for service traffic routing

questions.question: What is the container runtime interface (CRI)?
questions.role: K8s standard interface for container runtimes (containerd, CRI-O)

questions.question: What is CRI-O?
questions.role: Lightweight container runtime designed specifically for Kubernetes

questions.question: What is the K8s control loop?
questions.role: Continuously compare desired state to actual state and reconcile

questions.question: What is a K8s informer?
questions.role: Watch mechanism caching K8s resources and triggering handlers on change

questions.question: What is K8s workqueue?
questions.role: Rate-limited queue managing items to be processed by controller

questions.question: What is the leader election in K8s?
questions.role: Ensures only one instance of controller runs at a time in HA setup

questions.question: What is etcd raft consensus?
questions.role: Etcd uses Raft protocol requiring majority of nodes for writes

questions.question: What is K8s API server rate limiting?
questions.role: Limits requests per second to prevent API server overload

questions.question: What is a K8s webhook?
questions.role: HTTP callback extending K8s API (admission or conversion webhooks)

questions.question: What is mutating admission webhook?
questions.role: Modifies K8s objects before they're stored (inject sidecars)

questions.question: What is validating admission webhook?
questions.role: Validates K8s objects and can reject them before storage

questions.question: What is the difference between MutatingAdmissionWebhook and OPA Gatekeeper?
questions.role: Gatekeeper uses CRD-based policies via OPA; webhook is custom code

questions.question: What is pod preemption in K8s?
questions.role: Higher-priority pod evicts lower-priority pod to get resources

questions.question: What is pod disruption budget enforcement?
questions.role: K8s respects PDB during voluntary disruptions like node drains

questions.question: What is the purpose of node cordoning?
questions.role: Prevents new pods from being scheduled on a node (maintenance)

questions.question: What is the difference between cordon and drain?
questions.role: Cordon stops new scheduling; drain evicts existing pods too

questions.question: What is K8s horizontal pod autoscaler cool-down?
questions.role: Stabilization window preventing flapping: default 5min scale-down

questions.question: What is KEDA (Kubernetes Event-Driven Autoscaler)?
questions.role: Extends HPA with custom metrics from queues, databases, events

questions.question: What is CVE triage?
questions.role: Evaluating CVEs to determine relevance, exploitability, and priority

questions.question: What is the CVSS vector string?
questions.role: Encodes all CVSS metrics as compact string (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H)

questions.question: What is epss (exploit prediction scoring system)?
questions.role: Probability score for CVE being exploited in next 30 days

questions.question: What is VEX (Vulnerability Exploitability eXchange)?
questions.role: Statements asserting whether product is affected by specific CVE

questions.question: What is in-toto supply chain security?
questions.role: Defines and verifies each step in software supply chain

questions.question: What is the purpose of branch protection rules?
questions.role: Prevent direct pushes to protected branches, require reviews

questions.question: What is code owner review requirement?
questions.role: Require approval from code owners before merging (CODEOWNERS file)

questions.question: What is Dependabot security alerts?
questions.role: GitHub notifications about known vulnerabilities in dependencies

questions.question: What is SBOM attestation?
questions.role: Signed SBOM attached to container image for verifiable composition

questions.question: What is the NIST Cybersecurity Framework?
questions.role: 5-function framework: Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover

questions.question: What is the purpose of security headers in HTTP?
questions.role: Protect against XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing via response headers

questions.question: What is Content Security Policy (CSP)?
questions.role: HTTP header restricting sources for scripts, styles, images

questions.question: What is HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security)?
questions.role: Forces HTTPS connections, prevents SSL stripping attacks

questions.question: What is CORS in DevOps context?
questions.role: Server configuration allowing cross-origin requests from allowed origins

questions.question: What is certificate pinning?
questions.role: Hardcoding expected certificate fingerprint to prevent MitM even with valid cert

questions.question: What is credential stuffing?
questions.role: Using leaked username/password pairs to attack other services

questions.question: What is a rainbow table attack?
questions.role: Precomputed hash-to-password mappings for cracking hashed passwords

questions.question: What is salting passwords?
questions.role: Adding random value before hashing to prevent rainbow table attacks

questions.question: What is bcrypt advantage?
questions.role: Slow hashing algorithm with cost factor — expensive to brute force

questions.question: What is secrets sprawl?
questions.role: Secrets appearing in multiple unauthorized locations increasing exposure risk

questions.question: What is Docker's OCI (Open Container Initiative)?
questions.role: Industry standard for container image format and runtime specification

questions.question: What is the containerd vs Docker relationship?
