questions.role: Static method updating state based on props before render

questions.question: What is getSnapshotBeforeUpdate?
questions.role: Captures information from DOM before it is updated

questions.question: What is static getDerivedStateFromError?
questions.role: Updates state to show error fallback UI when child throws

questions.question: What is componentDidCatch?
questions.role: Catches errors in child components and logs them

questions.question: What is an Error Boundary?
questions.role: Class component catching JavaScript errors in child tree

questions.question: Why can't functional components be Error Boundaries?
questions.role: Error Boundaries require class lifecycle methods (not yet in hooks)

questions.question: What does the render method do in class components?
questions.role: Returns JSX describing what the UI should look like

questions.question: What is the constructor in a class component used for?
questions.role: Initialize state and bind event handlers

questions.question: What is super(props) in a class component constructor?
questions.role: Calls parent class (React.Component) constructor, required before this.props

questions.question: What happens if you call setState in componentDidUpdate without a condition?
questions.role: Infinite loop — it triggers another update

questions.question: What is the React lifecycle order on initial render?
questions.role: constructor → render → componentDidMount

questions.question: What triggers componentDidUpdate?
questions.role: State change or prop change after initial render

questions.question: What is the UNSAFE_ prefix on lifecycle methods?
questions.role: Methods being deprecated due to problems in async rendering

questions.question: What replaced componentWillMount?
questions.role: Either constructor or componentDidMount (WillMount was unreliable)

questions.question: What replaced componentWillReceiveProps?
questions.role: getDerivedStateFromProps (but it's rare to need it)

questions.question: What replaced componentWillUpdate?
questions.role: getSnapshotBeforeUpdate + componentDidUpdate

questions.question: In which lifecycle method should data fetching be done?
questions.role: componentDidMount (or useEffect with [])

questions.question: What is the re-render lifecycle order?
questions.role: render → getSnapshotBeforeUpdate → componentDidUpdate

questions.question: What does React.StrictMode do to lifecycle methods?
questions.role: Double-invokes certain methods in development to detect side effects

questions.question: When is componentWillUnmount called for a conditional component?
questions.role: When the condition becomes false and component is removed from tree

questions.question: What is the purpose of key prop in resetting lifecycle?
questions.role: Changing key forces full unmount + remount of component

questions.question: What is the fiber reconciler?
questions.role: React's internal engine managing component tree updates and scheduling

questions.question: What is concurrent mode in React 18?
questions.role: React can interrupt, pause, and resume rendering for better responsiveness

questions.question: What is Suspense for data fetching?
questions.role: Component that shows fallback while children wait for async data

questions.question: What is the difference between Suspense and Error Boundary?
questions.role: Suspense handles loading states; Error Boundary handles errors

questions.question: What is startTransition's effect on lifecycle?
questions.role: Defers non-urgent renders to keep high-priority updates fast

questions.question: What is tearing in concurrent rendering?
questions.role: UI showing inconsistent state because reads happen at different times

questions.question: What is useDeferredValue's lifecycle implication?
questions.role: Deferred value may lag behind actual value during transitions

questions.question: What is hydration in React?
questions.role: Attaching event listeners and React state to server-rendered HTML

questions.question: What is selective hydration?
questions.role: Hydrating the most urgent parts of the page first

questions.question: What is streaming SSR?
questions.role: Sending HTML in chunks as components are ready instead of all at once

questions.question: What is the Transition API's impact on rendering?
questions.role: Non-urgent state updates are interruptible and can be deprioritized

questions.question: What are React Server Components?
questions.role: Components that render on server without shipping their JS to client

questions.question: What is the difference between RSC and SSR?
questions.role: SSR renders to HTML string; RSC renders to a special React format with client interactivity

questions.question: What happens when a parent re-renders?
questions.role: All children re-render unless memoized

questions.question: What does React.memo's comparison function do?
questions.role: Custom function to determine if re-render is needed (true = skip)

questions.question: What is reconciliation?
questions.role: React's process of diffing virtual DOM to determine minimal DOM updates

questions.question: What is the virtual DOM?
questions.role: Lightweight in-memory representation of the actual DOM

questions.question: What does React batch lifecycle updates for?
questions.role: Performance — applying multiple state updates in one render pass

questions.question: What is the commit phase in React's lifecycle?
questions.role: Phase where React applies changes to the DOM

questions.question: What problem does Redux solve?
questions.role: Predictable state management for complex applications

questions.question: What are Redux's three core principles?
questions.role: Single source of truth, state is read-only, changes via pure functions

questions.question: What is a Redux action?
questions.role: Plain object with a type field describing an event

questions.question: What does store.dispatch() do?
questions.role: Sends an action to the store to trigger a state change

questions.question: What does store.getState() do?
questions.role: Returns the current state tree

questions.question: What does store.subscribe() do?
questions.role: Registers a callback called after every state change

questions.question: What is Redux middleware?
questions.role: Code that intercepts dispatched actions before they reach reducer

questions.question: What does applyMiddleware do?
questions.role: Composes middleware into the store enhancer

questions.question: What is redux-thunk?
questions.role: Middleware allowing action creators to return functions for async

questions.question: What is redux-saga?
questions.role: Middleware using generator functions to handle complex async flows

questions.question: What is a saga effect in redux-saga?
questions.role: Functions like call(), put(), take() describing async operations

questions.question: What is Redux Toolkit (RTK)?
questions.role: Official recommended toolset reducing Redux boilerplate

questions.question: What does configureStore from RTK do?
questions.role: Creates store with thunk middleware and DevTools preconfigured

questions.question: What does createSlice from RTK do?
questions.role: Generates action creators and reducer from a single config object

questions.question: What is the immer integration in RTK createSlice?
questions.role: Allows writing mutating code in reducers that produces immutable updates

questions.question: What is createAsyncThunk?
questions.role: RTK utility creating thunks with pending/fulfilled/rejected actions

questions.question: What lifecycle actions does createAsyncThunk generate?
questions.role: pending, fulfilled, rejected

questions.question: What is RTK Query?
questions.role: Data fetching and caching solution built into Redux Toolkit

questions.question: What is an endpoint in RTK Query?
questions.role: Definition of a specific API call (query or mutation)

questions.question: What is the difference between query and mutation in RTK Query?
questions.role: Query for reads, mutation for writes (POST/PUT/DELETE)

questions.question: What is cache invalidation in RTK Query?
questions.role: Invalidating cached data to trigger refetch when related data changes

questions.question: What is providesTags in RTK Query?
questions.role: Defines what data a query result provides for cache invalidation

questions.question: What is invalidatesTags in RTK Query?
questions.role: Tells RTK Query which cached data to refetch after a mutation

questions.question: What is useSelector hook?
questions.role: Extracts specific data from Redux store, re-renders on data change

questions.question: What is the equality check in useSelector?
questions.role: Reference equality by default — new object === re-render

questions.question: What is shallowEqual in react-redux?
questions.role: Shallow comparison for useSelector to avoid unnecessary re-renders

questions.question: What is useDispatch hook?
questions.role: Returns the dispatch function from Redux store

questions.question: What is the Provider component in react-redux?
questions.role: Makes Redux store available to all nested components via context

questions.question: What is normalizeState in Redux?
questions.role: Storing entities by ID in a flat structure to avoid nesting

questions.question: What is createEntityAdapter in RTK?
questions.role: Provides CRUD operations and normalized state structure for collections

questions.question: What is the selector pattern?
questions.role: Functions extracting derived data from Redux state

questions.question: What is createSelector from Reselect?
questions.role: Memoized selector recomputing only when inputs change

questions.question: What is the difference between Redux and Context?
questions.role: Redux has DevTools, middleware, immutability; Context is simpler

questions.question: When should you NOT use Redux?
questions.role: Simple apps with local state needs only

questions.question: What is the redux-persist library?
questions.role: Persists Redux state to localStorage/sessionStorage across sessions

questions.question: What is the devtools enhancer in RTK?
questions.role: Integrates Redux DevTools Extension for state/action inspection

questions.question: What is time-travel debugging in Redux?
questions.role: Replaying actions to debug state changes using DevTools

questions.question: What is the redux-observable library?
questions.role: Middleware using RxJS observables for async action streams

questions.question: What is an epic in redux-observable?
questions.role: Function taking and returning stream of actions via RxJS

questions.question: What is the flux standard action (FSA)?
questions.role: Convention for action objects: type, payload, error, meta

questions.question: What is an action payload?
questions.role: Data carried by an action to the reducer

questions.question: What is the command pattern in Redux actions?
questions.role: Actions describe what should happen (imperative: ADD_TODO)

questions.question: What is the event pattern in Redux actions?
questions.role: Actions describe what happened (past tense: TODO_ADDED)

questions.question: What is CQRS in context of Redux?
questions.role: Separating reads (selectors) from writes (actions/reducers)

questions.question: What is middleware composition in Redux?
questions.role: Chaining multiple middlewares: logger → thunk → saga

questions.question: What is the enhancer argument to createStore?
questions.role: Extends Redux with third-party capabilities (middleware)

questions.question: What is composeWithDevTools?
questions.role: Composes store enhancers with Redux DevTools support

questions.question: What is immutability important in Redux?
questions.role: Enables reliable change detection and time-travel debugging

questions.question: What is the downside of Redux?
questions.role: Significant boilerplate code (reduced by RTK)

questions.question: What does useMemo do?
questions.role: Memoizes expensive computed value, recomputes only on dependency change

questions.question: What does useCallback do?
questions.role: Memoizes function reference to prevent recreation on each render

questions.question: When should you use React.memo?
questions.role: When component re-renders often with same props and render is expensive

questions.question: What is premature optimization?
questions.role: Optimizing before identifying actual performance bottleneck

questions.question: What tool helps profile React performance?
questions.role: React DevTools Profiler

questions.question: What is code splitting?
questions.role: Splitting JS bundle into smaller chunks loaded on demand

questions.question: What is lazy loading in React?
questions.role: Loading components only when needed using React.lazy and Suspense

questions.question: What is virtualization (windowing) in React?
questions.role: Rendering only visible list items to improve performance of long lists

questions.question: What library implements list virtualization?
questions.role: react-virtual or react-window

questions.question: What is the cost of reconciliation?
questions.role: CPU time diffing virtual DOM to determine minimal real DOM updates

questions.question: What is a performance bottleneck common in React?
questions.role: Unnecessary re-renders caused by new object/function references

questions.question: What is the problem with creating objects in render?
questions.role: New reference on every render can break React.memo and cause child re-renders

questions.question: How do you solve new function reference in render?
questions.role: useCallback to memoize the function

questions.question: How do you solve new object reference in render?
questions.role: useMemo to memoize the object

questions.question: What is shouldComponentUpdate's role in optimization?
questions.role: Returns false to skip re-render when props/state haven't changed meaningfully

questions.question: What is the difference between React.memo and useMemo?
questions.role: React.memo memoizes component; useMemo memoizes a value inside component

questions.question: What is tree shaking?
questions.role: Removing unused exports from final bundle during build

questions.question: What is bundle analysis?
questions.role: Examining bundle composition to identify large dependencies

questions.question: What tool analyzes webpack bundle?
questions.role: webpack-bundle-analyzer

questions.question: What is the purpose of production build?
questions.role: Minification, dead code elimination, and optimizations (no dev warnings)

questions.question: What is Long Tasks in web performance?
questions.role: Tasks taking >50ms blocking the main thread

questions.question: What is Time to Interactive (TTI)?
questions.role: Time until page is reliably interactive for user

questions.question: What is First Contentful Paint (FCP)?
questions.role: Time when first DOM content is rendered on screen

questions.question: What is Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)?
questions.role: Time when largest visible element finishes rendering

questions.question: What is Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)?
questions.role: Measures unexpected visual layout shifts during page load

questions.question: What is the purpose of React.Profiler component?
questions.role: Measures rendering performance of its subtree

questions.question: What is turbo mode in React DevTools Profiler?
questions.role: Not a real feature — flame chart and ranked chart are actual views

questions.question: What is the impact of large component trees on performance?
questions.role: More reconciliation work per render cycle

questions.question: What is the purpose of key prop optimization?
questions.role: Stable keys prevent unnecessary unmount/remount of components

questions.question: What is event handler optimization?
questions.role: Defining handlers with useCallback to avoid new references on render

questions.question: What is context optimization?
questions.role: Split contexts to reduce number of components re-rendering on change

questions.question: What is the problem with putting everything in one Context?
questions.role: Any consumer re-renders when any part of context value changes

questions.question: What is context selector pattern?
questions.role: Using useMemo in provider or separate atoms to minimize re-renders

questions.question: What is image optimization in React?
questions.role: Using lazy loading, WebP format, responsive images, and next/image

questions.question: What is font loading optimization?
questions.role: Using font-display: swap and preloading critical fonts

questions.question: What is progressive hydration?
questions.role: Hydrating high-priority components first, deferring less important ones

questions.question: What is server-side rendering's performance benefit?
questions.role: Faster First Contentful Paint as HTML is ready before JS loads

questions.question: What is static site generation's performance benefit?
questions.role: Pre-built HTML at build time, served from CDN with minimal TTFB

questions.question: What is incremental static regeneration (ISR)?
questions.role: Next.js feature: regenerate static pages at runtime without full rebuild

questions.question: What is the purpose of a CDN?
questions.role: Serving static assets from geographically distributed servers for low latency

questions.question: What is resource preloading?
questions.role: <link rel='preload'> to fetch critical resources early

questions.question: What is prefetching?
questions.role: Loading resources for likely future navigations in advance

questions.question: What is service worker's role in performance?
questions.role: Caching assets for offline use and faster repeat visits

questions.question: What is memoizing API responses?
questions.role: Caching API responses to avoid duplicate network requests

questions.question: What is the React 18 concurrent rendering optimization?
questions.role: Keeps UI responsive by breaking rendering into interruptible units

questions.question: What is the useTransition optimization pattern?
questions.role: Wrapping non-urgent updates to keep input and critical UI responsive

questions.question: What is the purpose of React.StrictMode for optimization?
questions.role: Identifies potential issues early (double-invokes to detect side effects)

questions.question: What is component state colocation?
questions.role: Keeping state as close to where it is used as possible

questions.question: What is the render and commit phase optimization?
questions.role: React batches DOM writes in commit phase to minimize DOM operations

questions.question: What does React automatically optimize since version 19?
questions.role: Automatic memoization with React Compiler (formerly Forget)

questions.question: What is the most popular testing library for React?
questions.role: React Testing Library (RTL)

questions.question: What does React Testing Library encourage?
questions.role: Testing behavior from user's perspective, not implementation details

questions.question: What is Jest?
questions.role: JavaScript testing framework with test runner, assertions, and mocks

questions.question: What is the render() function in RTL?
questions.role: Renders component into a DOM for testing

questions.question: What is screen in RTL?
questions.role: Object providing query methods to find rendered elements

questions.question: What is getByText() in RTL?
questions.role: Finds element by its visible text content

questions.question: What is getByRole() in RTL?
questions.role: Finds element by its ARIA role (recommended approach)

questions.question: What is the difference between getBy and queryBy?
questions.role: getBy throws if not found; queryBy returns null

questions.question: What is findBy in RTL?
questions.role: Async query that waits for element to appear

questions.question: What is waitFor in RTL?
questions.role: Waits until assertion passes, retrying automatically

questions.question: What is a unit test?
questions.role: Tests a single component or function in isolation

questions.question: What is an integration test?
questions.role: Tests how multiple components work together

questions.question: What is an end-to-end (E2E) test?
questions.role: Tests complete user flows through the real browser/app

questions.question: What is the Testing Trophy?
questions.role: Most tests should be integration tests, fewer unit and E2E

questions.question: What is the Testing Pyramid?
questions.role: More unit tests than integration, fewer E2E (opposite of trophy)

questions.question: What is a mock function in Jest?
questions.role: jest.fn() — tracks calls, arguments, and can return specified values

questions.question: What is jest.spyOn?
questions.role: Creates a mock that also calls through to original implementation

questions.question: What is jest.mock()?
questions.role: Automatically mocks an entire module

questions.question: What is a snapshot test?
questions.role: Records component render output and alerts when it changes

questions.question: What is the downside of snapshot tests?
questions.role: They break on intentional UI changes and become unmaintained

questions.question: What is userEvent in RTL?
questions.role: Simulates real user interactions (clicks, typing) more accurately than fireEvent

questions.question: What is the difference between fireEvent and userEvent?
questions.role: userEvent simulates full browser user interaction; fireEvent dispatches a DOM event

questions.question: What is a test coverage report?
questions.role: Shows percentage of code exercised by tests

questions.question: What is 100% code coverage a guarantee of?
questions.role: Nothing — tests can pass with full coverage but miss important cases

questions.question: What is the AAA pattern in testing?
questions.role: Arrange, Act, Assert — structure for organizing test code

questions.question: What is a test double?
questions.role: Generic term for mocks, stubs, spies, and fakes used in testing

questions.question: What is the difference between stub and mock?
questions.role: Stub provides canned responses; mock also verifies interactions

questions.question: What is Cypress?
questions.role: E2E testing framework running tests in real browser

questions.question: What is Playwright?
questions.role: Microsoft's cross-browser E2E testing framework

questions.question: What is Vitest?
questions.role: Jest-compatible test runner optimized for Vite projects

questions.question: What is test isolation?
questions.role: Each test runs independently without side effects from other tests

questions.question: What is beforeEach in Jest?
questions.role: Runs setup code before each test in a describe block

questions.question: What is afterEach in Jest?
questions.role: Runs cleanup code after each test

questions.question: What is beforeAll in Jest?
questions.role: Runs once before all tests in a describe block

questions.question: What is describe in Jest?
questions.role: Groups related tests together

questions.question: What is the it/test function in Jest?
questions.role: Defines a single test case

questions.question: How do you test async code in Jest?
questions.role: Return a promise, use async/await, or use done callback

questions.question: What is msw (Mock Service Worker)?
questions.role: Intercepts network requests in tests using service worker

questions.question: Why is testing implementation details bad?
questions.role: Tests break on refactoring even when behavior is unchanged

questions.question: What is a query priority in RTL?
questions.role: Order of preference: role > label > placeholder > text > ID

questions.question: What does ByLabelText query do?
questions.role: Finds form element associated with label text

questions.question: What does ByPlaceholderText query do?
questions.role: Finds input by its placeholder attribute

questions.question: What is data-testid attribute?
questions.role: Custom attribute for selecting test elements as last resort

questions.question: Why should data-testid be a last resort?
questions.role: It tests implementation, not user behavior

questions.question: What is testing a hook?
questions.role: Using renderHook() from RTL to test custom hooks in isolation

questions.question: What does renderHook return?
questions.role: Object with result.current (hook's return value) and other utilities

questions.question: What is act() wrapper in React testing?
questions.role: Ensures all state updates and effects are applied before assertions

questions.question: When is act() required?
questions.role: When triggering state updates outside React's event system

questions.question: What is axe-core used for?
questions.role: Automated accessibility testing in Jest/RTL tests

questions.question: What is storybook?
questions.role: UI component explorer for developing and testing components in isolation

questions.question: What is visual regression testing?
questions.role: Comparing component screenshots to detect unintended visual changes

questions.question: What is a React component?
questions.role: Reusable, self-contained UI piece returning JSX

questions.question: What are the two types of React components?
questions.role: Functional components and Class components

questions.question: What is a functional component?
questions.role: JavaScript function that accepts props and returns JSX

questions.question: What is a class component?
questions.role: ES6 class extending React.Component with render method

questions.question: What are props?
questions.role: Data passed from parent to child component

questions.question: What is the difference between props and state?
questions.role: Props are external (passed in); state is internal (managed by component)

questions.question: Can props be modified by the child component?
questions.role: No — props are read-only

questions.question: What is a Higher Order Component (HOC)?
questions.role: Function that takes a component and returns an enhanced component

questions.question: What is the Compound Components pattern?
questions.role: Multiple components working together sharing implicit state via context

questions.question: What is the Render Props pattern?
questions.role: Component accepting a function prop that returns JSX to render

questions.question: What is the Container/Presentational component pattern?
questions.role: Separating data logic (container) from UI (presentational)

questions.question: What are controlled components?
questions.role: Components where form state is managed by React state

questions.question: What are uncontrolled components?
questions.role: Components where form state is managed by the DOM

questions.question: What is the children prop?
questions.role: Special prop containing JSX passed between component tags

questions.question: What is React.Children.map?
questions.role: Iterate over children with proper handling of edge cases

questions.question: What is React.cloneElement used for?
questions.role: Clone element and add/override props (used in compound components)

questions.question: What is prop validation?
questions.role: Defining expected types for props using PropTypes or TypeScript

questions.question: What is defaultProps?
questions.role: Sets default values for props when they are not provided

questions.question: What is the purpose of displayName on a component?
questions.role: Improves debugging in React DevTools and error messages

questions.question: What is forwardRef used for?
questions.role: Forwarding a ref from parent through a component to its DOM element

questions.question: What is useImperativeHandle used with?
questions.role: forwardRef — to customize what ref exposes to parent

questions.question: What is a pure component?
questions.role: Component returning same output for same input, no side effects

questions.question: What is React.Fragment?
questions.role: Wrapper that renders no DOM node, allowing multiple root elements

questions.question: What is the shorthand for React.Fragment?
questions.role: <> </> (empty angle brackets)

questions.question: What is a Portal in React?
questions.role: Renders children into a different DOM node outside current tree

questions.question: Why use React portals?
questions.role: To render modals, tooltips above z-index of parent without CSS hacks

questions.question: What is Error Boundary?
questions.role: Class component catching errors in child tree and showing fallback

questions.question: What is getDerivedStateFromError?
questions.role: Static method updating state to show error fallback UI

questions.question: What are Client Components in Next.js App Router?
questions.role: Components with 'use client' directive that run in browser

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'use client' directive?
questions.role: Marks component and its imports as client-side bundle boundary

questions.question: What is component composition?
questions.role: Building complex UI by combining smaller focused components

questions.question: Why prefer composition over inheritance in React?
questions.role: React's component model is built for composition, not class hierarchies

questions.question: What is the headless component pattern?
questions.role: Components providing logic/behavior without UI (often via render props/hooks)

questions.question: What is polymorphic component?
questions.role: Component accepting 'as' prop to render as different HTML element

questions.question: What is controlled vs uncontrolled component design?
questions.role: Controlled: caller manages state; Uncontrolled: component manages own state

questions.question: What is a slot pattern?
questions.role: Named children via props for flexible layout composition (like <header> slot)

questions.question: What are named exports vs default export for components?
questions.role: Named allows multiple exports per file; default is the main one

questions.question: What is component co-location?
questions.role: Keeping related code (tests, styles, hooks) near the component file

questions.question: What is index.js barrel file?
questions.role: Single file re-exporting from a folder for cleaner imports

questions.question: What is the atomic design methodology?
questions.role: Organizing components as Atoms, Molecules, Organisms, Templates, Pages

questions.question: What is a design system?
questions.role: Collection of reusable components with consistent design and behavior rules

questions.question: What is Storybook used for?
questions.role: Building and documenting components in isolation outside the app

questions.question: What is component-driven development?
questions.role: Building UI from components up, driven by Storybook or similar

questions.question: What is a form component pattern?
questions.role: Encapsulating form logic with validation and submission handling

questions.question: What is a wizard/multi-step form pattern?
questions.role: Series of components tracking current step and form state

questions.question: What is infinite scroll vs pagination?
questions.role: Infinite loads more on scroll; pagination shows discrete pages

questions.question: What is a virtual list component?
questions.role: Renders only visible items of a large list for performance

questions.question: What is skeleton loader?
questions.role: Placeholder UI matching layout while real content loads

questions.question: useState setter signature is setState(newValue) or:
questions.role: setState(prev => newVal) functional update form

questions.question: What is the problem with setTimeout inside useEffect reading state?
questions.role: Captures stale state from when effect ran — use ref or functional update

questions.question: How do you read latest state inside a setTimeout?
questions.role: Use a ref updated by useEffect to always have latest value

questions.question: What is a ref-based event handler pattern?
questions.role: Store handler in ref so it always reads fresh state without dependencies

questions.question: What does useCallback's dependency array contain?
questions.role: All values from component scope used inside the callback

questions.question: What is the identity of a setState function from useState?
questions.role: Stable — same reference across all renders (no need in deps)

questions.question: What is the identity of dispatch from useReducer?
questions.role: Stable — same reference across renders

questions.question: What is the identity of context value?
questions.role: New reference when Provider re-renders — can cause consumer re-renders

questions.question: What is the trick to stabilize context value?
questions.role: useMemo for the context value object in Provider

questions.question: What is a hook that returns both state and ref?
questions.role: Custom hook syncing a ref to current state value for stale closure fix

questions.question: What is the problem with using a function as default state?
questions.role: Function is called on every render without lazy init — use () => fn()

questions.question: What is the use hook for context?
questions.role: use(MyContext) — reads context, can be conditional (React 19)

questions.question: What is the use hook for promises?
questions.role: use(promise) — suspends component until promise resolves (React 19)

questions.question: What is cache() in React 19?
questions.role: Memoizes function results across renders (server component caching)

questions.question: What is the purpose of react-query's queryClient?
questions.role: Central store for all query caches — shared across app via QueryClientProvider

questions.question: What is useQueryClient hook?
questions.role: Access queryClient instance to imperatively fetch, invalidate, or update cache

questions.question: What is prefetchQuery?
questions.role: Loads query data before it's needed for instant display on navigation

questions.question: What is the difference between useQuery and useSuspenseQuery?
questions.role: useSuspenseQuery integrates with Suspense boundary for loading state

questions.question: What is the window focus refetch in React Query?
questions.role: Refetches stale queries when window/tab regains focus

questions.question: What is the network reconnect refetch?
questions.role: Refetches offline queries when network connection restores

questions.question: What is staleTime: Infinity in React Query?
questions.role: Data is never considered stale — cached forever until explicitly invalidated

questions.question: What is gcTime (garbage collect time) in React Query?
questions.role: Time unused cache entries are kept before being removed

questions.question: What is the useIsFetching hook?
questions.role: Returns number of queries currently fetching (global loading indicator)

questions.question: What is the useIsMutating hook?
questions.role: Returns number of mutations currently running

questions.question: What is the onSettled callback?
questions.role: Called after mutation succeeds or fails (cleanup regardless of outcome)

questions.question: What is suspense mode in React Query?
questions.role: Integrates with Suspense for loading state instead of isLoading flag

questions.question: What is the select option for transforming data?
questions.role: Transforms/derives data from cache without storing transformed version

questions.question: What is the placeholderData option?
questions.role: Shows data while fetching without treating it as real cached data

questions.question: What is the notifyOnChangeProps option?
questions.role: Limits which prop changes trigger re-render (e.g., only 'data')

questions.question: What is the keepPreviousData option?
questions.role: Shows old data while new query loads (good for pagination)

questions.question: What is zustand's subscribe method?
questions.role: Subscribes to store changes outside React components

questions.question: What is zustand's getState method?
questions.role: Reads current state outside React component (non-reactive)

questions.question: What is zustand's setState equivalent?
questions.role: The set function: store.setState({key: value}) or set(fn)

questions.question: What is the shallow helper in zustand?
questions.role: Equality function for useStore to prevent re-render on structural equality

questions.question: What is a slice pattern in zustand?
questions.role: Split large store into smaller slice creators combined with spread

questions.question: What is zustand's devtools middleware?
questions.role: Connects store to Redux DevTools for inspection and time-travel

questions.question: What is zustand's persist middleware?
questions.role: Automatically persists store to localStorage/sessionStorage

questions.question: What is the immer middleware in zustand?
questions.role: Allows writing mutating draft updates in zustand actions

questions.question: What is Jotai's atomWithStorage?
questions.role: Atom that persists its value in localStorage/sessionStorage

questions.question: What is Jotai's atomWithReset?
questions.role: Atom that can be reset to initial value via useResetAtom hook

questions.question: What is Jotai's atomWithReducer?
questions.role: Atom with reducer function for complex state transitions

questions.question: What is Jotai's useAtomValue?
questions.role: Read-only atom hook (no setter returned)

questions.question: What is Jotai's useSetAtom?
questions.role: Write-only atom hook (no value returned) — avoids re-render on read

questions.question: What is Jotai's atomFamily cleanup?
questions.role: Remove atoms from family to prevent memory leaks

questions.question: What is the selectAtom in Jotai?
questions.role: Derive a read-only atom from another atom with a selector function

questions.question: What is splitAtom in Jotai?
questions.role: Split array atom into individual atoms for each element

questions.question: What is atomWithObservable in Jotai?
questions.role: Creates atom from RxJS observable for reactive streams

questions.question: What is focusAtom in jotai-optics?
questions.role: Creates lens-focused atom for accessing nested state properties

questions.question: What is Redux Toolkit's createListenerMiddleware?
questions.role: Lightweight alternative to redux-saga for event-driven side effects

questions.question: What is startListening in createListenerMiddleware?
questions.role: Registers a listener to react to specific actions

questions.question: What is the takeLatest effect in createListenerMiddleware?
questions.role: Cancels previous listener run when same action fires again

questions.question: What is RTK's createDynamicMiddleware?
questions.role: Add middleware to store after initialization (for code splitting)

questions.question: What is the asyncThunkCreator pattern?
questions.role: Create thunks with consistent error handling and loading states

questions.question: What is the createAppAsyncThunk?
questions.role: Typed version of createAsyncThunk with app's RootState and AppDispatch

questions.question: What is Redux Toolkit's combineSlices?
questions.role: Lazily combines slices — adds them as they're imported (code splitting)

questions.question: What is the injectInto method on slices?
questions.role: Injects slice into a combined reducer at runtime

questions.question: What is the purpose of selector memoization?
questions.role: Avoid recomputing derived state when inputs haven't changed

questions.question: What is the defaultMemoize in reselect?
questions.role: Caches only last result (size 1) — may re-compute on rotation

questions.question: What is unstable_serialize in TanStack Query?
questions.role: Deterministic query key serialization for consistent cache keys

questions.question: What is the queryOptions helper in TanStack Query v5?
questions.role: Type-safe helper for defining shared query configurations

questions.question: What is the output of rendering undefined in JSX?
questions.role: Renders nothing (undefined is not rendered)

questions.question: What is the output of rendering false in JSX?
questions.role: Renders nothing (false is a falsy value that React skips)

questions.question: What is the output of rendering an empty string in JSX?
questions.role: Renders an empty text node (empty string is rendered unlike false)

questions.question: What is the output of rendering 0 in JSX?
questions.role: Renders the number 0 (0 is rendered unlike false/null/undefined)

questions.question: What is the correct fix for the 0 rendering issue?
questions.role: Convert to boolean: {count > 0 && <Comp />} or {!!count && <Comp />}

questions.question: What is JSX's handling of object rendering?
questions.role: Throws error: 'Objects are not valid as a React child'

questions.question: What is the toString trick for objects in JSX?
questions.role: Call .toString() or JSON.stringify() to convert to string for display

questions.question: What is the purpose of React.isValidElement?
questions.role: Checks if a value is a valid React element before rendering

questions.question: What is the children as function pattern?
questions.role:
Render prop: {(data) =>
{data}
}

questions.question: What is React.Children.forEach?
questions.role: Iterates children without creating new array (no return value)

questions.question: What is the cloneElement use case for injecting props?
questions.role: Parent injects additional props into children: React.cloneElement(child, extraProps)

questions.question: What is forwardRef's render function signature?
questions.role: (props, ref) => JSX — receives both props and forwarded ref

questions.question: What is the ref in forwardRef used for?
questions.role: Attaching to DOM element or passing to child component's forwardRef

questions.question: What is the constraint on forwardRef?
questions.role: Can only forward one ref — for multiple refs use useImperativeHandle

questions.question: What is the key as identity in component resetting?
questions.role: Same component at same key = preserve state; different key = reset state

questions.question: What is the purpose of aria-hidden?
questions.role: Hides element from accessibility tree (decorative/redundant elements)

questions.question: What is role=button for non-button elements?
questions.role: Adds button semantics to non-button element (also needs keyboard handlers)

questions.question: What is the tabIndex=-1 use case?
questions.role: Makes element focusable by code but not in tab order

questions.question: What is the tabIndex=0 use case?
questions.role: Adds element to natural tab order based on DOM position

questions.question: What is tabIndex positive number problem?
questions.role: Overrides natural tab order — creates confusing keyboard experience

questions.question: What is the onBlur event in React?
questions.role: Fires when element loses focus (bubbles unlike native blur)

questions.question: What is onFocus event bubbling in React?
questions.role: React's onFocus bubbles (unlike native focus event) — use for delegation

questions.question: What is the autoComplete attribute?
questions.role: Hints browser for autofill suggestions ('on', 'off', 'email', etc.)

questions.question: What is the inputMode attribute?
questions.role: Hints mobile keyboard type (numeric, email, tel) without changing input type

questions.question: What is the pattern attribute on input?
questions.role: Regex pattern for native HTML validation

questions.question: What is the min/max on number input?
questions.role: Sets minimum and maximum accepted values

questions.question: What is the step attribute on number input?
questions.role: Sets increment amount for up/down arrows and validation

questions.question: What is the accept attribute on file input?
questions.role: Filters file types in file picker dialog (.jpg, image/*)

questions.question: What is the multiple attribute on file input?
questions.role: Allows selecting multiple files at once

questions.question: What is the capture attribute on mobile?
questions.role: Hints to open camera directly: capture='user' or capture='environment'

questions.question: What is the history stack limit?
questions.role: Browsers typically limit history stack to 50 entries

questions.question: What is the difference between a and Link?
questions.role: <a> triggers full page reload; <Link> uses client-side navigation

questions.question: What is the to prop on Link?
questions.role: Destination path, object, or function for navigation

questions.question: What is the state prop on Link?
questions.role: Passes state to next location accessible via useLocation().state

questions.question: What is the replace prop on Link?
questions.role: Replaces current history entry instead of pushing

questions.question: What is the relative='path' vs relative='route' on Link?
questions.role: path: relative to current URL; route: relative to parent route

questions.question: What is the unstable_viewTransition on Link?
questions.role: Enables View Transitions API for animated page transitions

questions.question: What is the purpose of RouterProvider vs BrowserRouter?
questions.role: RouterProvider uses data router (loaders/actions); BrowserRouter is simpler

questions.question: What is the future flag in createBrowserRouter?
questions.role: Opt into future behavior changes before they become default

questions.question: What is the v7_startTransition flag?
questions.role: Wraps state updates in startTransition for smoother navigation

questions.question: What is the v7_relativeSplatPath flag?
questions.role: Changes how relative paths work inside splat routes

questions.question: What is HydrateFallback in React Router?
questions.role: Component shown while waiting for data during initial hydration

questions.question: What is the clientLoader in Remix/React Router?
questions.role: Runs in browser instead of server for client-side data fetching

questions.question: What is the clientAction in Remix?
questions.role: Form action running in browser instead of server

questions.question: What is resource route in Remix?
questions.role: Route without UI component — serves JSON, files, or API responses

questions.question: What is Remix's useFetcher().load?
questions.role: Loads data from a route's loader without navigating

questions.question: What is the optimistic UI with useFetcher?
questions.role: Show expected result while fetcher is in submitting state

questions.question: What is the purpose of shouldRevalidate returning false?
questions.role: Skip loader rerun after navigation when data hasn't changed

questions.question: What is the purpose of the meta export in Remix?
questions.role: Define meta tags (title, description) for each route

questions.question: What is the links export in Remix?
questions.role: Add link tags (stylesheets, preloads) for each route

questions.question: What is the headers export in Remix?
questions.role: Set HTTP response headers for a route

questions.question: What is the loader return type in Remix?
questions.role: Any serializable value or Response object (json() helper)

questions.question: What is the json() helper in Remix?
questions.role: Creates JSON Response with proper headers

questions.question: What is the redirect() in Remix loaders?
questions.role: Throws Response that redirects user to new URL

questions.question: What is the throw redirect pattern?
questions.role: throw redirect('/login') in loader stops execution and redirects

questions.question: What is form method='get' vs 'post' in Remix?
questions.role: GET triggers loader; POST triggers action

questions.question: What is the fetcher.data in Remix?
questions.role: Data returned from the last fetcher submission or load

questions.question: What is the fetcher.formData?
questions.role: FormData from the in-flight submission (for optimistic UI)

questions.question: What is revalidation in Remix?
questions.role: After mutation, all visible route loaders automatically re-run

questions.question: What is the shouldRevalidate's actionResult argument?
questions.role: Data returned from action used to decide if revalidation needed

questions.question: What is the React element pool?
questions.role: React reuses element objects — why mutations are dangerous

questions.question: What is the current tree in React Fiber?
questions.role: Tree of fibers representing currently rendered UI

questions.question: What is the alternate fiber?
questions.role: Work-in-progress counterpart to current fiber — swapped on commit

questions.question: What is the effectTag/flags in React Fiber?
questions.role: Bit flags marking fibers for work: placement, update, deletion

questions.question: What is the subtreeFlags optimization?
questions.role: Aggregates child effect flags to skip subtrees with no work

questions.question: What is the beginWork phase?
questions.role: Processes fiber and its children — creates or updates child fibers

questions.question: What is the completeWork phase?
questions.role: Creates DOM nodes and builds effect list bottom-up

questions.question: What is the commitWork phase?
questions.role: Applies all mutations to DOM after rendering is complete

questions.question: What are the three sub-phases of commit?
questions.role: beforeMutation, mutation, layout — each traverses effect list once

questions.question: What is the passive effect phase?
questions.role: Where useEffect callbacks run (after browser paint)

questions.question: What is the layout effect phase?
questions.role: Where useLayoutEffect callbacks run (synchronously after mutation)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Scheduler package?
questions.role: Implements cooperative multitasking for React's concurrent features

questions.question: What is the message channel trick in Scheduler?
questions.role: Uses MessageChannel to yield to browser between render units

questions.question: What is time slicing?
questions.role: Breaking long renders into 5ms chunks allowing browser to interrupt

questions.question: What is the expiration time in React (old model)?
questions.role: Deadline-based priority — replaced by lanes in React 18

questions.question: What is the lanes model?
questions.role: Bitmask-based priority system in React 18 replacing expiration times

questions.question: What is SyncLane?
questions.role: Highest priority lane for synchronous user interactions

questions.question: What is TransitionLane?
questions.role: Lower priority lane for non-urgent state updates (via startTransition)

questions.question: What is IdleLane?
questions.role: Lowest priority lane — only processes when nothing else needs doing

questions.question: What is entanglement in lanes?
questions.role: Some lanes must be processed together — can't process one without others

questions.question: What is batchedUpdates in legacy React?
questions.role: Manually group updates outside React event handlers (pre-React 18)

questions.question: What is the problem flushSync solves?
questions.role: Forces synchronous re-render for code that needs DOM updates immediately

questions.question: What is the root.render() replacement?
questions.role: ReactDOM.createRoot(el).render(<App/>) replaces ReactDOM.render() in React 18

questions.question: What is hydrateRoot vs createRoot?
questions.role: hydrateRoot attaches to server-rendered HTML; createRoot starts fresh

questions.question: What is the onRecoverableError option in createRoot?
questions.role: Callback for errors React auto-recovers from during hydration

questions.question: What is the identifierPrefix option in createRoot?
questions.role: Prefix for IDs generated by useId to avoid conflicts in micro-frontends

questions.question: What is the React 19 act() behavior change?
questions.role: act() now wraps async operations by default for better test utilities

questions.question: What is the useFormState/useActionState difference?
questions.role: useActionState (React 19) replaces useFormState from React DOM

questions.question: What is the ref as prop in React 19?
questions.role: Function components can accept ref as regular prop (no forwardRef needed)

questions.question: What is the cleanup function for ref in React 19?
questions.role: Ref callback can return cleanup function called on unmount

questions.question: What is the Reselect v5 change?
questions.role: weakMapMemoize as default (unlimited cache); createSelectorCreator more flexible

questions.question: What is lruMemoize in Reselect?
questions.role: Memoizer with limited cache size (least recently used eviction)

questions.question: What is createSelectorCreator(weakMapMemoize)?
questions.role: Creates selector with WeakMap memoization (default in v5)

questions.question: What is unstable_autotrackMemoize?
questions.role: Experimental memoizer tracking accessed state for optimal re-computation

questions.question: What is RTK's combineSlices lazy loading?
questions.role: Dynamically injects slice reducers when feature code loads

questions.question: What is RTK's createAction with prepare callback?
questions.role: Customize action payload: createAction('type', (arg) => ({payload: transform(arg)}))

questions.question: What is the payloadCreator in createAsyncThunk?
questions.role: Function returning data or throwing error for thunk result

questions.question: What is the getThunkAPI argument in createAsyncThunk?
questions.role: Object with dispatch, getState, extra, signal, rejectWithValue

questions.question: What is rejectWithValue in createAsyncThunk?
questions.role: Returns a rejected action with custom error payload (not throws)

questions.question: What is the condition option in createAsyncThunk?
questions.role: Cancels thunk before starting if condition returns false

questions.question: What is the signal in createAsyncThunk?
questions.role: AbortController signal for canceling in-flight async operations

questions.question: What is abort() on thunk dispatch return?
questions.role: Cancels dispatched thunk if it's still pending

questions.question: What is RTK Query's transformResponse?
questions.role: Transforms raw API response before storing in cache

questions.question: What is RTK Query's transformErrorResponse?
questions.role: Transforms error response to custom format before storing

questions.question: What is the merge option in RTK Query?
questions.role: Custom merge function for paginated data (combining responses)

questions.question: What is the serializeQueryArgs option?
questions.role: Custom serialization for query arguments as cache key

questions.question: What is RTK Query's queryFn?
questions.role: Override default fetch with custom async function for endpoints

questions.question: What is the onCacheEntryAdded lifecycle?
questions.role: Run side effects when a cache entry is created (e.g., WebSocket subscription)

questions.question: What is cacheDataLoaded in onCacheEntryAdded?
questions.role: Promise resolving when cache has first value — await to start subscription

questions.question: What is cacheEntryRemoved in onCacheEntryAdded?
questions.role: Promise resolving when cache entry is removed — for cleanup

questions.question: What is the enhanceEndpoints utility?
questions.role: Add tags or other options to endpoints after createApi

questions.question: What is the API slice's middleware?
questions.role: Required RTK Query middleware handling cache lifecycle

questions.question: What is RTK Query refetchOnMountOrArgChange?
questions.role: Force refetch when component mounts or args change (bypass cache)

questions.question: What is RTK Query automatic polling?
questions.role: pollingInterval on useQuery refetches at regular intervals

questions.question: What is the skipToken for RTK Query?
questions.role: import {skipToken} — passes as arg to skip query without changing hook call

questions.question: What is RTK Query's lazy query?
questions.role: useLazyQuery — manually trigger fetch instead of auto-fetching on mount

questions.question: What is RTKQ's streaming updates?
questions.role: onCacheEntryAdded + WebSocket for real-time cache updates

questions.question: What is the invalidation cascade in RTK Query?
questions.role: Mutation invalidating tag forces all queries with that tag to refetch

questions.question: What is the optimistic update undo in RTK Query?
questions.role: Catch error in onQueryStarted and call patchResult.undo() to revert

questions.question: What is the focused invalidation in RTK Query?
questions.role: Invalidate specific {type, id} instead of all entries of that type

questions.question: What is the bundle analyzer webpack plugin?
questions.role: Visualizes webpack bundle composition as interactive treemap

questions.question: What is source-map-explorer?
questions.role: Analyzes bundle size by source file using source maps

questions.question: What is rollup-plugin-visualizer?
questions.role: Bundle size visualization for Vite/Rollup projects

questions.question: What is the vite-bundle-visualizer?
questions.role: Analyzes Vite bundle composition and sizes

questions.question: What is next/bundle-analyzer?
questions.role: Official Next.js plugin for analyzing bundle sizes

questions.question: What is the performance budget?
questions.role: Limits on bundle size, load time, or other metrics that fail build if exceeded

questions.question: What is the performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory API?
questions.role: Measures memory usage of current web page (Chrome 89+)

questions.question: What is the Memory tab in Chrome DevTools?
questions.role: Profile heap snapshots and memory allocation for leak detection

questions.question: What is a memory leak detection strategy in React?
questions.role: Take heap snapshots before/after actions and compare retained objects

questions.question: What is the reason React.memo doesn't help with context?
questions.role: Context changes bypass memo — component re-renders regardless

questions.question: What is the useMemo for expensive renders pattern?
questions.role: Memoize JSX output: const jsx = useMemo(() => <Exp />, [dep])

questions.question: What is the windowing threshold?
questions.role: Rule of thumb: virtualize lists with 50+ items that are expensive to render

questions.question: What is overscan in list virtualization?
questions.role: Rendering extra items above/below viewport to prevent blank during scroll

questions.question: What is dynamic row height in virtualization?
questions.role: Variable-size list where each item has different height

questions.question: What is TanStack Virtual?
questions.role: Headless virtualization for any scrollable element (rows, columns, grids)

questions.question: What is the intersection observer for lazy images?

questions.question: What is next/image optimization?
questions.role: Automatic WebP conversion, lazy loading, size optimization, blur placeholder

questions.question: What is the blurDataURL prop?

questions.question: What is the sizes attribute on images?

questions.question: What is the loading='lazy' attribute?

questions.question: What is a CSS containment?

questions.question: What is content-visibility: auto?

questions.question: What is the render-blocking resource?
questions.role: CSS and synchronous JS in head blocking first paint

questions.question: What is the async attribute on script?

questions.question: What is the defer attribute on script?
questions.role: Loads in parallel, executes after HTML parsing (in order)

questions.question: What is font subsetting?

questions.question: What is variable font?

questions.question: What is font-display: swap?

questions.question: What is resource hint: preconnect?

questions.question: What is mockImplementation in Jest?

questions.question: What is mockImplementationOnce?

questions.question: What is jest.spyOn with mockRestore?
questions.role: Restores original implementation after test (used with afterEach)

questions.question: What is the moduleNameMapper in Jest config?

questions.question: What is the transform config in Jest?
questions.role: Specifies how to transform files before testing (babel-jest, ts-jest)

questions.question: What is ts-jest?
questions.role: Jest preprocessor that compiles TypeScript during testing

questions.question: What is @swc/jest?
questions.role: Fast SWC-based Jest transformer (faster than babel-jest)

questions.question: What is the globals config in Jest?
questions.role: Sets test environment globals (e.g., structuredClone, fetch)

questions.question: What is testEnvironment in Jest?

questions.question: What is setupFilesAfterFramework in Jest?

questions.question: What is globalSetup/globalTeardown in Jest?

questions.question: What is --testPathPattern in Jest?
questions.role: Filter which test files to run by regex pattern

questions.question: What is --watch mode in Jest?
questions.role: Re-runs tests related to changed files automatically

questions.question: What is --coverage in Jest?
questions.role: Generates code coverage report after test run

questions.question: What is the coverage threshold in Jest?
questions.role: Fail tests if coverage drops below configured percentage

questions.question: What is Istanbul (nyc)?
questions.role: Code coverage library used by Jest internally

questions.question: What is a coverage report format?
questions.role: lcov, json, html, text — configure in Jest coverageReporters

questions.question: What is the purpose of data-testid as last resort?
questions.role: When no role, label, or text uniquely identifies element

questions.question: What is the getByTestId query?
questions.role: Find element by data-testid attribute (use sparingly)

questions.question: What is the ByRole accessibility name?
questions.role: Role + accessible name (from aria-label, aria-labelledby, text content)

questions.role: ARIA spec algorithm for determining element's accessible name for screen readers

questions.question: What is the expanded option in ByRole query?

questions.question: What is the pressed option in ByRole query?

questions.question: What is the checked option in ByRole query?

questions.question: What is the current option in ByRole query?

questions.question: What is @testing-library/react testing Server Components?
questions.role: Not directly testable — test via integration tests or mock the server

questions.question: What is the builder pattern for React components?
questions.role: Using method chaining to construct complex component configurations

questions.question: What is the factory pattern for React?
questions.role: Function creating components with shared configuration

questions.question: What is component intrinsic element types in TypeScript?
questions.role: React.JSX.IntrinsicElements defining all valid HTML element prop types

questions.question: What is ComponentRef type in React 19?
questions.role: Extracts ref type from a component: ComponentRef<typeof Button>

questions.question: What is ComponentPropsWithRef?
questions.role: Props type including ref for components that accept refs

questions.question: What is ComponentPropsWithoutRef?
questions.role: Props type explicitly excluding ref

questions.question: What is the RefObject vs MutableRefObject?
questions.role: RefObject is read-only .current; MutableRefObject allows writing

questions.question: What is React.HTMLAttributes?
questions.role: Base type with all common HTML attributes (className, onClick, style, etc.)

questions.question: What is the extends HTMLAttributes pattern?
questions.role: interface MyProps extends React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> — inherits all HTML props

questions.question: What is the satisfies operator for component config?
questions.role: Validates object matches type while preserving literal types

questions.question: What is a compound component example (Tabs)?
questions.role: <Tabs><Tabs.List><Tabs.Tab/></Tabs.List><Tabs.Panel/></Tabs>

questions.question: What is the Disclosure pattern?
questions.role: Show/hide pattern with trigger and content components sharing state

questions.question: What is the Combobox pattern?
questions.role: Input + listbox combo with keyboard navigation and ARIA

questions.question: What is the dialog/modal pattern in React?
questions.role: Portal + focus trap + escape key + aria-modal + backdrop click to close

questions.question: What is focus trap?
questions.role: Library preventing focus from leaving modal/dialog for accessibility

questions.question: What is react-focus-trap-zone?
questions.role: Component trapping keyboard focus within boundaries for accessibility

questions.question: What is the toast notification pattern?
questions.role: Global notification system using context + portal for app-wide messages

questions.question: What is the use-toast hook pattern?
questions.role: Custom hook exposing toast API throughout app via context

questions.question: What is the command palette pattern?

questions.question: P: The internet has revolutionized communication. Q: Today, information can be shared globally in seconds. R: Before its invention, letters took weeks to travel between continents. S: This transformation has had both positive and negative consequences. Arrange P, Q, R, S in logical order:
questions.role: P-R-Q-S

questions.question: P: Exercise is essential for maintaining good health. Q: Even a 30-minute daily walk can significantly reduce the risk of chronic diseases. R: Many people, however, lead sedentary lifestyles due to desk jobs. S: It strengthens the heart, improves mood, and increases longevity. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-S-R-Q

questions.question: P: Water is the most precious resource on Earth. Q: Without it, no life can survive. R: Yet, billions of people lack access to clean drinking water. S: Conservation and responsible usage are therefore critical. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-R-S

questions.question: P: Education is the foundation of any civilized society. Q: It enables individuals to think critically and contribute meaningfully. R: Governments must therefore prioritize funding for public schools. S: Without educated citizens, democracy itself is at risk. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-S-R

questions.question: P: Social media has connected people across the globe. Q: However, it has also become a platform for misinformation and cyberbullying. R: Users must therefore develop digital literacy skills. S: The key is to use it responsibly and critically. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-R-S

questions.question: P: Meditation has been practiced for thousands of years. Q: Modern science now confirms its benefits for mental health. R: It reduces stress, improves focus, and promotes emotional well-being. S: Even a few minutes of daily meditation can make a noticeable difference. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-R-S

questions.question: P: The Amazon rainforest is often called the lungs of the Earth. Q: It produces approximately 20% of the world's oxygen. R: Rapid deforestation threatens not only wildlife but global climate stability. S: Protecting it must be a global priority. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-R-S

questions.question: P: Space exploration has yielded tremendous scientific knowledge. Q: Satellites now provide global navigation, weather forecasting, and communication. R: Despite the enormous costs involved, the benefits are undeniable. S: Future missions aim to establish human settlements on Mars. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-R-S

questions.question: P: Reading regularly enhances vocabulary and critical thinking. Q: It also reduces stress by providing an escape from daily pressures. R: Children who read from a young age develop stronger academic skills. S: Encouraging a reading habit is one of the greatest gifts parents can give. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: R-P-Q-S

questions.question: P: Plastic pollution is a growing environmental crisis. Q: Millions of tons of plastic waste end up in oceans each year. R: Marine animals mistake plastic debris for food, often fatally. S: Reducing single-use plastic is an urgent global necessity. Arrange in logical order:
questions.role: P-Q-R-S
